# Segment 8, Phase 1 — the BALANCED two-concept SOURCE probe (phase `s8_phase1_symmetric_probe`)

**Goal.** Close the single reviewer-flagged gap in Segment-8's centerpiece: run the **balanced
two-concept-2AFC external linear SOURCE probe** — both candidate concepts c,d present in the prompt, ONE
injected, an external **linear** probe reads **which co-present concept was injected**, on **held-out
concept pairs**, with salience controlled — the representational analogue of the behavioral
controlled-2AFC / 5a opposed test. Phase-0 (`write_up_s8_mechanistic_probe.md`) ran the one-concept
matched-text probe and flagged this balanced design (recommended by GPT/Gemini in
`results/s8_probe_design_review.md`) as unrun. Charter: `INSTRUCTIONS.md`; pre-registration
`prereg_s8_phase1.md` (committed before results, pre-commit hash `1ef5076`). Model: `Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct`.
**STATUS: COMPLETE.**

## HEADLINE (the negative — a NON-IDENTIFIABILITY result, honestly scoped)
**There is NO separately-decodable prompt-vs-injection SOURCE signal in the residual: "which co-present
concept was injected" is NON-IDENTIFIABLE from "which co-present concept is more internally active" (the
already-characterized relative-magnitude cue), and no concept-general SOURCE tag is decodable beyond that.**
The load-bearing grounds (corrected after a measurement-validity review found two of my initial empirical
pillars were artifacts — see "Corrections" below):
1. **Non-identifiability (the load-bearing, probe-independent argument).** The injected concept is
   **always** the more-internally-active one — its projection-z along its own diff-of-means direction is
   ~14σ (synthetic) / ~4σ (patch) while the *other* co-present concept stays ~0 **even when it is
   text-emphasized** (at the effective WORKING strength mean |z_injected − z_other| ≈ 27/14/8σ at
   L32/40/48; frac < 2σ ≈ 0). So a concept-conditioned MAGNITUDE model on [z_opt1, z_opt2] decodes which
   was injected at **1.00** (= the proj-z oracle), leaving **NO residual headroom** for a separate source
   signal at the strength where the injection is effective. Lowering the strength to shrink the gap (the
   half-alpha arm) just lowers the injection toward inert — and there the held-out source probe still stays
   ≤ the proj-z predictor on the salience-matched subset (Results). You cannot construct a trial where
   source ≠ relative-internal-magnitude (an injection that does not raise internal activation is not an
   injection).
   "Which was injected" ≡ "which is more internally active" = the **S6 §6C bounded positive
   (relative magnitude), NOT source.**
2. **The readout HAS power; the held-out source-probe signal that exists is the MAGNITUDE/ANOMALY
   bounded-positive, NOT source.** A held-out probe distinguishing "a concept was injected" from "no
   injection" (NON-relational) is **0.93–1.00** — the readout is not dead. The held-out which-of-two SOURCE
   probe reaches **0.65–0.66 (linear) / 0.71 (MLP)** even in the cleanest **output-presence-MATCHED** decisive
   arm (half-alpha symmetric), beating chance + the output floor. **But it is NOT source:** it stays BELOW
   the concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE predictor at every synthetic cell (source−oracle = −0.14 to −0.44) and ≤ the
   proj-z predictor on the pre-registered salience-matched subset; it is the OFF-MANIFOLD synthetic arm
   (anomaly by the charter, not source); and — output matched — it is the INTERNAL projection-z bounded
   positive (S6 §6C, the charter-§4 "must not be sold as source" cell).
3. **The on-manifold (source-clean) injection cannot even host the balanced design** (with the available
   primitive). The full-context sequence patch is an all-or-nothing graft: grafting concept c **ERASES**
   the other co-present concept d (d output-presence → 0.06; d text-emphasis → 0.00; d internal proj-z →
   negative). So the only both-concepts-preserving injection is the off-manifold synthetic one.
4. **Phase-0** (the one-concept matched-text probe) is the powered, non-degenerate held-out source test:
   the prompt-vs-injection boundary there is concept-agnostic graft/anomaly (transfer 1.0), NOT source.

Together these complete the project's representational negative: prompt-vs-injection source is not
separable from the relative-magnitude / anomaly signature in any identifiable balanced contrast we could
construct, matching the behavioral 5a/2AFC null. This is a **non-identifiability bound**, NOT a
metaphysical "source is provably absent" (see Bounds).

![balanced source probe](../plots/s8b_probe.png)
![layer profile](../plots/s8b_layers.png)

## Design (what was actually constructible — the central methodological move)

A **design-verify** (`results/graded_s8b_verify.jsonl`) measured, in the two-concept setting, the realized
output-presence of BOTH concepts under each injection, deciding what is constructible:
- **On-manifold patch (the "decisive" injection per the charter) cannot host the balanced design** — it
  overwrites the host concept (erasure above). Captured anyway (symmetric + opposed) to document this; it
  degenerates to the Phase-0 single-concept matched-text result (the residual ≈ the injected concept,
  held-out probe at chance).
- **Synthetic additive injection preserves both concepts** → the workhorse. Off-manifold ⇒ a positive
  there would be anomaly-contaminated; but it is the **best case for a positive**, so its negative is the
  strongest possible.

**The probe (`run_s8b_capture.py` → `analyze_s8b_probe.py`).** 20 cross-category NON-style pairs (40
concepts; each in exactly one pair ⇒ 5-fold GroupKFold-by-pair = held-out-by-concept, no leak). Residuals
captured (no generation) under the neutral-continuation meta at READOUT_LAYERS {28,32,40,48,56,63} (>L=24,
off-by-one) at mean_ctx (meta-independent internal context state) + last (unmodified-position propagated
effect). Label = **positional** (was opt1 [first-listed] or opt2 [second-listed] injected), counterbalanced
over order ⇒ concept-general. Arms: synthetic **aligned** (3a; symmetric, inject one at WORKING), synthetic
**output-MATCHED** (3b presence-matched; half-alpha symmetric — injected output-presence ≈ the other's, the
NON-degenerate decisive arm), synthetic **opposed** (3b; emphasize the NON-injected, inject one; WORKING +
half-alpha — these turned out degenerate for the positional probe, see Corrections), patch aligned/opposed
(erasure), no-injection (floors). Pair-clustered bootstrap 95% CIs.

**The decisive floors (the source claim must beat ALL).** chance (0.5); **no-injection** (each
residual duplicated under both labels → 0.50; + the trained probe applied to no-injection → ~0.50);
**internal-projection-z predictor** (oracle argmax over the two concepts' z, using the concept directions —
the relative-magnitude cue); **matched-capacity SALIENCE probe** (identical LR/features, label = the
higher-proj-z option — the fair, same-capacity "source beyond magnitude" test, reviewer-endorsed);
**output-presence predictor** (argmax graded presence); **salience-mimic** (a probe trained on
text-emphasized no-injection prompts to predict the emphasized option).

## Results (held-out balanced accuracy; `results/s8b_probe_summary.json`)

- **Positive control (readout power):** a held-out probe detecting "a concept was injected" vs "no
  injection" (non-relational) = **0.93 (synthetic) / 1.00 (patch)** → the readout has real power.
- **Concept-conditioned MAGNITUDE model [z_opt1, z_opt2] = 1.00** = the proj-z oracle: with the two
  candidate directions, which was injected is perfectly decodable via relative internal magnitude. At full
  (WORKING) strength mean |z_injected − z_other| ≈ 27/14/8σ (L32/40/48), frac < 2σ ≈ 0 (magnitude at
  ceiling, no headroom); at half strength (`syn_sym_half`) the z-separation shrinks (frac < 2σ up to 0.71
  at L63), enabling the salience-matched-subset test below — where the source probe stays ≤ the proj-z
  predictor.
- **Held-out which-of-two SOURCE probe — the two NON-degenerate arms (symmetric context depends on order):**
  - **aligned (3a, output NOT matched):** source 0.59 [0.53, 0.66] at mean_ctx L40.
  - **output-MATCHED (3b presence-matched: half-alpha SYMMETRIC, a POST-HOC arm added after the opposed arm
    proved degenerate; injected output-presence 1.82 ≈ other 1.62, output-oracle→injected 0.58):** the
    source probe reaches **0.65–0.66 (linear) / 0.71 (MLP) across L40–L56** — a REAL above-chance signal
    that beats chance, the output-presence floor (0.58), and (noisily, at deep layers) the crude argmax-z
    salience-LABEL probe. **This is NOT source**, on four converging grounds, each pre-registered:
    1. **It never beats the concept-aware MAGNITUDE floor** — the proj-z predictor (0.92–1.00) exceeds it at
       EVERY synthetic cell (source−proj-z = −0.14 to −0.44; the patch arm's apparent +0.05 is the erasure case — its oracle is uninformative since the second concept is gone, leaving only the Phase-0 graft/anomaly); on the **pre-registered salience-matched subset**
       (|z_inj−z_other| < 2σ, n=201–342 at L48–L63) the source probe (0.63–0.67) is ≤ the proj-z predictor
       (0.66–0.94), gap significantly negative at L48/L56 (−0.26 [−0.34,−0.18] / −0.17 [−0.27,−0.05]) and
       indistinguishable at L63 (where the injection's proj-z signature has decayed) — it reads only (part
       of) the relative-internal-magnitude cue, never significantly beyond it.
    2. It is the **OFF-MANIFOLD SYNTHETIC** arm — per the charter (§3 caveat 1) any synthetic separability is
       **anomaly, not source** (the decisive source test must be on-manifold; where the source probe beats
       the argmax-z label probe at L56, it is reading the injection's off-manifold spike — anomaly — not a
       source tag; and it still sits below the concept-aware magnitude oracle).
    3. **Output-presence is matched**, so the signal is the **INTERNAL projection-z / anomaly axis (the S6
       §6C bounded positive), NOT source** — exactly the charter-§4 "must not be sold as source" cell.
    4. The same-capacity salience-label-probe comparison is NOISY (source−salience: −0.01 at L40, +0.05 at
       L56) because argmax-z is a noisy binarized proxy near-matched — so we do NOT rest any claim on it.
    ⇒ the decodable signal even in the cleanest presence-matched arm is the relative-internal-magnitude +
    off-manifold-anomaly bounded positive, NOT source.
- **The OPPOSED arms are DEGENERATE BY CONSTRUCTION (not a source null):** the opposed context depends only
  on (emphasized, injected), so pooling order puts the **byte-identical residual under both labels** → the
  held-out positional probe (and the auto-0.50 salience-mimic / no-injection-both-labels floors, which
  share this structure) is forced to exactly 0.50. These cells carry **no information about source** and
  are NOT cited as evidence. (Verified: `syn_opp_work|cat__freedom|o1|cat` == `...|o2|cat`.)
- **The two salience axes DO dissociate (a genuine finding, read via the oracles):** in the **opposed-½**
  arm the **output-presence axis points to the EMPHASIZED (non-injected)** concept (output-oracle→injected
  = 0.22) while the **internal-proj-z axis points to the injected** concept — exactly the behavioral 5a
  structure (the model follows prompt-text/output salience; internally the injection is louder). Sampled
  continuations in the opposed-½ arm talk about the EMPHASIZED concept, not the injected one
  (`results/s8b_data_inspection.md` §6).
- **Patch erasure documented** (design-verify): the full-context graft drives the other concept's
  output-presence to 0.06 (symmetric) / 0.00 (emphasized) and its internal proj-z negative — it overwrites
  the host, so it cannot host two co-present concepts (with this primitive).

## Corrections (two research-plan-reviewer passes caught artifacts/overclaims in my initial framing)
My first write cited empirical "pillars" that the reviewer correctly flagged; I corrected them and
re-grounded the verdict on the **concept-aware MAGNITUDE-oracle floor** (which holds at every synthetic cell) + the
salience-matched subset + the off-manifold/non-identifiability bounds:
- **"SOURCE ≈ SALIENCE probe (gap≈0) confirms no source"** is unsound: where the proj-z oracle = 1.0
  (z-separation large) the salience label ≡ the injected label so gap≈0 is **definitional**; where the
  injection has decayed (deep layers) the argmax-z label is a **noisy** proxy and the source probe even
  *beats* the salience-label probe (e.g. +0.05 at L56) by reading the off-manifold synthetic anomaly —
  still **anomaly, not source**, and still below the magnitude oracle. So no claim rests on the salience-
  probe gap; the load-bearing floor is **source < the concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE predictor** (true at
  every non-degenerate synthetic cell) + **source ≤ proj-z on the salience-matched subset**.
- **"Opposed-arm 0.50 = source null"** is a **construction artifact** (identical states under both labels;
  see above), NOT evidence — the opposed arm is non-informative for the held-out positional probe. The
  **non-degenerate** decisive arm is the post-hoc output-MATCHED `syn_sym_half`.
- **"transfer-to-no-injection / floors clean = no order artifact"** was inaccurate: `transfer_to_none`
  ranges 0.0–0.99 and the salience-mimic / both-labels floors are auto-0.50 (cannot detect a leak). The
  real readout-validity check is the **positive control** (detect injection vs none, 0.93–1.00).
- **"salience-matched subset unrealizable"** holds only for the WORKING arm (z-sep 27/14/8σ); it IS
  realizable in the half-alpha `syn_sym_half` arm (frac<2σ = 0.15/0.42/0.56/0.71 at L40/48/56/63) — so the
  pre-registered matched-subset test was RUN there (source ≤ proj-z on the matched trials).
- Scoped "no on-manifold two-concept state exists" → "**not constructible with the full-context sequence-
  patch primitive tested**" (sub-span / interpolated grafts untried; a noted future direction).

## Verdict (pre-registered NEGATIVE)
Prompt-vs-injection SOURCE is **not decodable beyond the relative-internal-magnitude / off-manifold-anomaly
bounded-positive** in the balanced two-concept design. The held-out source probe DOES carry an above-chance
signal (0.65–0.71 in the output-matched arm) — the readout has power (positive control 0.93–1.00) — but at
**every** non-degenerate SYNTHETIC cell that signal stays **below the concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE
predictor** (source−oracle = −0.14 to −0.44) and, on the **pre-registered salience-matched subset**, the
source probe is ≤ the proj-z predictor: it reads (part of) the relative-internal-magnitude cue + the off-manifold synthetic
anomaly, **never beyond magnitude**, and — with output-presence matched — it is specifically the INTERNAL
projection-z (S6 §6C) cue, the charter-§4 "must not be sold as source" outcome. Combined with the
**non-identifiability** (injection necessarily raises internal magnitude, so source ≡ relative-internal-
magnitude cannot be dissociated — the proj-z oracle is at ceiling where z-separation is large), the
**on-manifold non-constructibility** (the patch erases the host), and **Phase-0** (the powered on-manifold
one-concept probe: source = concept-agnostic graft/anomaly, not source), the verdict is a NEGATIVE: no
concept-general SOURCE tag is decodable beyond relative magnitude / anomaly. The representational analogue
of the behavioral 5a/2AFC null. **Honest scope:** the pre-registered POSITIVE (probe reads the injected
concept ABOVE the magnitude floor) was, in retrospect, unreachable by construction (injection ≡ raising
internal magnitude). The Segment-8 representational verdict = Phase-0 + Phase-1, both negative.

## What this licenses (and the over-claims avoided)
- ✅ **Non-identifiability bound (the honest claim):** "which co-present concept was injected" is not
  separable from "which is more internally active" (relative magnitude) in any identifiable balanced
  contrast we could construct; no concept-general SOURCE tag is decodable beyond relative magnitude /
  graft-anomaly. Union with Phase-0 (the powered one-concept matched-text probe: source = concept-agnostic
  graft/anomaly, not source) ⇒ a robust representational negative.
- ❌ NOT "airtight EMPIRICAL separation" / NOT "the source probe is at chance" — the source probe carries a
  real above-chance signal (0.65–0.71, output-matched), and the decisive opposed arm was degenerate by
  construction. The negative rests on: **source < the concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE oracle at every
  non-degenerate cell** (+ ≤ proj-z on the salience-matched subset), the **non-identifiability** argument,
  the **positive control** (the readout has power), the **off-manifold/anomaly** charter caveat on the
  synthetic arm, the **patch erasure**, and **Phase-0** — NOT on the opposed-arm 0.50 or the salience-probe
  gap (which is definitional where z-sep is large and a noisy argmax-z artifact where it is small).
- ❌ NOT "source is represented but not used" — no source beyond magnitude is decodable; the magnitude
  model's / proj-z oracle's success is the relative-magnitude cue, not source.
- ❌ NOT a metaphysical "source is not represented anywhere": carried **bounds** — (i) **non-identifiability
  by construction** (injection ≡ raising internal magnitude, so the design cannot, even in principle,
  dissociate source from relative magnitude — a positive was unreachable); (ii) LINEAR / single-MLP probe ≠
  all probes / ≠ after training; **source-as-relational-binding** may be non-linear or in attention/QK
  circuits (outside a single-token residual readout); (iii) **off-manifold non-identifiability** (the only
  both-concepts-preserving injection is synthetic; the on-manifold patch cannot host the design with the
  primitive tested — sub-span/interpolated grafts untried); (iv) **OOD-disruption** (the synthetic
  injection could mask an on-manifold source subspace). 32B / this injection method / this readout.

## Judgment-heavy choices (documented; reviewed by query_all_models + a research-plan-reviewer subagent)
- **Synthetic-as-the-only-constructible-injection + document the on-manifold patch's non-constructibility**
  (vs forcing an on-manifold balanced design). Forced by the empirical erasure (verify); endorsed by all 3
  frontier reviewers ("the full-context sequence patch overwrites the host; document it as a finding; do
  not edit GPU code"). Scoped to the primitive tested (sub-span/interpolated grafts untried).
- **The MAGNITUDE-oracle floor + the non-identifiability ARGUMENT as load-bearing** (vs the salience-probe
  gap / the opposed arm). Two reviewer passes found the salience-probe gap is **definitional** where z-sep
  is large (and a noisy argmax-z artifact where small) and the opposed arm is **degenerate by
  construction** — so the negative rests on **source < the concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE oracle at every
  non-degenerate cell** (+ ≤ proj-z on the salience-matched subset) + the non-identifiability argument +
  the positive control + the off-manifold/anomaly caveat + the patch erasure + Phase-0. Reported in
  "Corrections."
- **Positional, counterbalanced, held-out-by-pair label** (concept-general). The opposed-context choice
  made this label degenerate (identical states under both labels); the **non-degenerate** evaluable arms
  are the symmetric ones (aligned 0.59; output-MATCHED `syn_sym_half` 0.65–0.71 — above the absolute bar
  but below the magnitude oracle, so the bounded-positive, not source). The proper readout-validity check
  is the **positive control** (detect injection vs none, held-out, 0.93–1.00), not the auto-0.50 floors.
- **Neutral readout meta** (not the 2AFC question) so the readout is the intrinsic residual state, not the
  model's forced answer (avoids circularity with the behavioral 2AFC); mean_ctx (internal) + last
  (unmodified) positions; full readout-layer sweep. **A non-linear MLP bound** confirms no non-linear
  source beyond magnitude on the evaluable arm.

## Recommendation for Segment 9 (synthesis)
The expected negative obtains, as a **non-identifiability result**. **S9 should consolidate: "prompt-vs-
injection SOURCE is not separable from relative salience / internal-magnitude / anomaly in any identifiable
contrast — a non-identifiability bound, not a metaphysical absence"** — the Segment-8 representational
verdict is the **union of Phase-0 (the powered one-concept matched-text probe: source = concept-agnostic
graft/anomaly, not source) + Phase-1 (balanced two-concept: source ≡ relative internal magnitude is
non-identifiable — injection necessarily raises internal magnitude, so the design cannot dissociate the
two; the on-manifold injection cannot even host two co-present concepts)**. This sharpens the behavioral
negative at the representational level: the model's inability to attribute source is not merely a
self-report limit — source is not separable from the relative-magnitude/anomaly signature. **Guards (carry
forward):** S9 must NOT harden this into an unbounded "source isn't there" (the non-identifiability is
by-construction; linear/MLP-probe, off-manifold-non-identifiability, OOD, and relational/attention bounds
all stand), and must NOT cite the degenerate opposed-arm 0.50 or the definitional salience-gap as
empirical evidence (see Corrections).

See `progress_log_s8_phase1_symmetric_probe.md` for the chronology + data inspection; `prereg_s8_phase1.md`
for the pre-registration; `results/s8b_probe_summary.json` for all numbers;
`results/s8b_data_inspection.md` for inspected examples; `results/s8_phase1_design_review.md` for the
3-model design review.

  {cat|freedom, fire|pirate, bridge|fear, ocean|betrayal}, generated NEUTRAL continuations (base
  primed system, no source/2AFC question) under each two-concept injection config, graded BOTH the
  injected concept's AND the other (co-present) concept's realized output-presence. Goal: decide what
  salience configurations the balanced probe can actually use (does the on-manifold patch erase the
  other concept; can synthetic preserve both / be salience-controlled).

### Key findings (results/graded_s8b_verify.jsonl; both-concept output-presence, n=16/cell)
| ctxmode   | arm           | inj(A) | other(B) | reading |
|-----------|---------------|--------|----------|---------|
| symmetric | none          | 1.875  | 2.000    | both present ~equal (baseline) |
| symmetric | patch_b0.5    | 1.938  | **0.062**| **patch ERASES the other concept** |
| symmetric | patch_b1.0    | 2.125  | **0.062**| patch erases other (all-or-nothing, full-context graft) |
| symmetric | syn_sub       | 1.938  | 1.812    | synthetic PRESERVES both |
| symmetric | syn_work_x0.5 | 2.000  | 1.875    | both present, A≈B (output-matched) |
| symmetric | syn_work_x1.0 | 2.812  | 0.062    | high alpha: injected A dominates output |
| emphB     | none          | 0.188  | 1.812    | emphasis works (B≫A) |
| emphB     | patch_b1.0    | 2.188  | **0.000**| patch erases the emphasized B too |
| emphB     | syn_work_x0.5 | 1.375  | 2.188    | **opposed**: both present, emphasized B louder (output) |
| emphB     | syn_work_x1.0 | 2.688  | 0.250    | injected A dominates (S5 replication: 2.78 vs 0.97) |

### Decisive design implications (the central methodological finding)
- **The on-manifold sequence patch CANNOT host the balanced two-concept design**: it is a full-context,
  all-or-nothing graft, so grafting concept A's donor over the context ERASES the other co-present
  concept B (other-presence → 0.06; emphasis → 0.00). There is no on-manifold injected state with TWO
  co-present concepts. This is the representational form of the S5/Phase-0 non-identifiability bound
  (a coherent on-manifold injected state IS a graft and overwrites the recipient).
- **Only the synthetic (additive) injection preserves both concepts** → the balanced probe is
  constructible ONLY with synthetic injection (off-manifold → a probe positive there is contaminated by
  the off-manifold internal-projection/anomaly asymmetry, NOT source; per `INSTRUCTIONS.md` §3 this is
  flagged and NOT headlined). This makes the synthetic balanced probe the **best case for a positive**
  (it preserves both concepts AND adds a clean, easily-detected concept-direction spike): if even THAT
  cannot read source above the internal-magnitude / output-presence floors held-out, the negative is
  airtight.
- **Salience axes (output-presence) are controllable with synthetic alpha:** symmetric syn_work_x0.5 is
  output-matched (A≈B); emphB syn_work_x0.5 is opposed (emphasized B louder on output than injected A).
  But the **internal projection-z** axis still leans to the injected concept (the synthetic adds its
  vector) — so the decisive test is whether the probe beats the **internal-projection-z predictor**
  (§4), not just the output-presence one. Both predictors are reported.

### Notes / next
- The on-manifold patch is still captured in the main run (symmetric + opposed) to DOCUMENT the erasure
  (it degenerates to the Phase-0 matched-text single-concept result) — giving it its shot per the charter.
- Next: pre-register (`prereg_s8_phase1.md`), then the main residual capture + held-out probe.
- Cost this checkpoint: verify gen ~$1.13 Modal + grading ~$2.47 Opus.

   salience-mimic / no-injection-both-labels floors share this degeneracy (can't detect a leak).

   constructible trial (the injection mechanically raises the injected concept's proj-z by ~14sigma), the

## Reproducibility verification (`--assert-cached`)

The production pipeline reruns from cache at the layers that matter for the headline numbers:

- **Analysis / plotting layer** (`analyze_*.py`, `plot_*.py`, `plot_final.py`): regenerates every summary
  JSON and figure from the committed `results/*.jsonl`/`*.json` with **no API calls** — verified for
  `analyze_s5`, `analyze_s6`, `analyze_s7`, `analyze_s8_probe --design matched_text`, `analyze_s8b_probe`,
  and `plot_final`.
- **Grading layer** (the paid Opus layer; `run_grade_*.py --assert-cached`): **fully cache-clean — $0.00**
  — verified across segments (`run_grade_salience` on s5/s7 steerscans, `run_grade_coherence` on s6,
  `run_grade_introspect` on s8 deny, `run_grade_s8b_present`). This confirms the committed `gen_*.jsonl`
  are intact and every grader call reproduces from cache.
- **GPU-generation layer** (`run_*.py --assert-cached`): the generation **outputs** are materialized as the
  committed `results/gen_*.jsonl`; re-running `--assert-cached` now **misses on the `image_id` field only**.
  The Modal cache key is `{model_id, method, kwargs, image_id, code_hash, sample_idx, this_call_uuid}`
  (`modal_runner.py`), where `image_id = image.object_id`. Entering `app.run()` re-resolved a **new**
  container image build id (`im-PhHQoiIREvcGc5jwW7Alra`) that differs from the id baked into the cached
  entries — an environment-drift artifact in the **container-image identity**, not a change in the
  model-side code (`code_hash` = `88851b869ea239d5`, unchanged), the inputs, or the results. No
  `steering_modal.py` edit was made (which would change `code_hash` and invalidate all Modal caches), and
  no committed S0–S8 GPU result was re-run.

**Net:** every headline number is computed by the cache-clean analysis layer from the committed
`gen_*.jsonl` / `graded_*.jsonl`, and the paid grading layer is fully `--assert-cached` clean. The
GPU-generation `image_id` miss does **not** affect any headline number (the outputs are committed and
unchanged); it is the project's documented "bit-exact GPU reproduction is not the norm; `--assert-cached`
reproduces the cached outputs" caveat, here at the level of Modal's image-object identity.

## Segment-8 Phase-1 outcome (one line)
The BALANCED two-concept source probe gives a NON-IDENTIFIABILITY negative: "which co-present concept was
injected" is non-identifiable from "which is more internally active" (injection necessarily raises internal
proj-z ~14sigma vs ~0), so a concept-conditioned magnitude model decodes it at ceiling with no source
headroom; a POSITIVE CONTROL (detect injection vs none, held-out) is 0.93-1.00 so the readout has power.
The held-out which-of-two source probe DOES reach 0.65-0.71 in the output-MATCHED non-degenerate arm
(syn_sym_half) but stays BELOW the concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE oracle at every synthetic cell (source-projz
-0.14..-0.35) and <= proj-z on the salience-matched subset => relative-internal-magnitude + off-manifold
anomaly, NOT source. The on-manifold patch ERASES the other concept (can't host the design); Phase-0 is
the powered on-manifold source test (negative). Completes the Segment-8 representational verdict (= Phase-0 + Phase-1). Stay on 32B. Next: S9.

## CRITICAL Phase-1 gotcha (a review caught this; FIXED in framing, not data)
- **The OPPOSED arm's held-out positional probe is DEGENERATE BY CONSTRUCTION** (forced 0.50): the opposed
  context `emphasize_ctx(emph,inj)` depends only on (emphasized, injected), NOT on opt1/opt2 order, so
  pooling order puts the SAME residual under both labels. Do NOT cite opposed-arm 0.50 as a source null.
  The auto-0.50 salience-mimic / no-injection-both-labels floors share this (can't detect leaks) -> use the
  POSITIVE CONTROL (detect_injection) instead. The matched-capacity salience-probe GAP is DEFINITIONAL
  (injected==higher-proj-z) -> not independent evidence. The load-bearing claim is the NON-IDENTIFIABILITY
  ARGUMENT + Phase-0 + the positive control + the patch erasure. (See write-up "Corrections" section.)

## Segment-8 Phase-1 gotchas / tribal knowledge
- **The on-manifold sequence patch is a FULL-CONTEXT, all-or-nothing graft -> it ERASES the other
  co-present concept** (other-presence -> 0.06 sym / 0.00 emph; proj-z negative). So the balanced
  two-concept design is constructible ONLY with the synthetic additive injection (preserves both). This is
  the central design constraint (a non-identifiability finding); do NOT try to force an on-manifold
  balanced design without a NEW sub-span patch primitive (editing steering_modal.py invalidates all Modal
  caches — avoid; reviewers agreed to document the erasure instead).
- **Source == relative-internal-magnitude (non-identifiable):** the injection mechanically makes the
  injected concept the more-internally-active one (z ~14sigma), so you cannot construct a salience-matched
  trial where source != magnitude. The decisive test is the **matched-capacity SALIENCE probe** (same
  LR/features, label=higher-proj-z option): SOURCE probe ~= SALIENCE probe everywhere -> magnitude, not source.
- **Held-out concept-blind probes are capacity-limited for relational binding** (they predict ~constant per
  held-out pair -> exactly 0.50 on opposed/none/salience-mimic). The matched-capacity salience-probe
  comparison + the non-identifiability argument control for this (both probes equally limited and equal).
- **The two salience axes dissociate (use BOTH):** output-presence (the load-bearing behavioral axis) vs
  internal proj-z (S6 6C). In opposed-1/2 output->emphasized, internal->injected; report both oracles.
- Same cold-start safetensors race at concurrency >=10 (Modal reschedules; output complete; verify with
  `--assert-cached`). Concurrency ~14 fine. `*.npz` git-ignored (regenerable); `total_cost.jsonl` force-committed.

## Segment-8 gotchas / tribal knowledge
- **The probe MUST be matched-text.** The asymmetric route-P(concept-context)-vs-route-A(neutral-context) design
  is dominated by the PROMPT-TEXT confound (a linear probe reads "is the concept literally in the prompt" =
  re-reading the prompt; ALL floors saturate at ~1.00). Put the concept in the prompt for ALL conditions and vary
  only the injection. (Design review: `results/s8_probe_design_review.md`.)
- **Off-by-one:** the injection is a `forward_pre_hook` on block L, so `hidden_states[L]` (collected BEFORE block
  L runs) does NOT reflect it — read at L>L (READOUT_LAYERS start at 28). Reading at/below L makes the injection
  routes byte-identical to the un-injected context (verified).
- **Patch β is all-or-nothing in output-presence** (β≤0.4→~0.1; β≥0.6→~2.0). The only coherent, output-present,
  on-manifold concept graft is β=1 (presence ~1.96 = matched to the prompt's ~1.9). K1 (S5) reconfirmed: the
  prompt route floors high (~1.6-1.9), so WORKING is the genuinely presence-matched point (no true sub point).
- **Deny direction (DETECT−DENY diff-of-means) is valid** (traintest_cos 0.86) but ablating it (projection-out)
  is bounded by the existing projection — it removes the report axis (lowering P(Yes)) rather than flipping the
  deny default. The additive form drives P(Yes) up for everything (yes-bias). Validate the direction via the
  additive contrast before interpreting the ablation.
- **Transient cold-start safetensors race** ("Runner terminated" / `UntypedStorage`) at concurrency ≥10 — Modal
  reschedules; output is complete (verify with `--assert-cached`). Concurrency ~12-14 is fine.
- **sklearn/scipy were pip-installed into the `.venv`** (not present initially) for the local probe.

## Segment-7 outcome (one line)
The 32B source-attribution negative **GENERALIZES** to BOTH `meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct` (different
family + more capable) AND `Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct` (scale-up within family): 5a opposed picks-injected
**< 0.5 every layer** (max 0.495 / 0.458), none survive BH-FDR; the injection is demonstrably EFFECTIVE
(aligned 0.70/0.74; matched-norm-random presence ~0.1) yet never overrides the prompt; same non-source
structure (B1 salience grows/peaks with depth; on-manifold patch read "prompt" despite presence ~2.0 =
anomaly); detection output-presence-gated, sub≈0. Supports the STRUCTURAL limiter, not a capability limit.

## Run a model end-to-end (driver)
`scripts_s7_cross_model/run_model_pipeline.sh {lib,calib,battery}` runs the full library+calibration+battery
pipeline for a model. Usage: `STEER_MODEL_ID=... TAG=... BLAYERS=8,16,24,32,40,48,56 bash scripts_s7_cross_
model/run_model_pipeline.sh lib` (then `calib`, then `battery`); then `analyze_s7.py` + `plot_s7.py` +
`inspect_s7.py` + `run_s7_grader_spotcheck.py` (all read TAG from model_profile). To add a new model: add a
profile entry to `model_profile.py` and run the three stages. Pick `BLAYERS` from the calibration's coherent
band (`match_layers_summary_<tag>.json` -> `n_coherent_reachable`).

## How to run the new model (the model-parameterized pipeline)
- **Pick the model via env (Modal does NOT forward local env — it is baked into `image.env`, so set it
  locally and it propagates):** `export STEER_MODEL_ID="meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct" STEER_GPU_COUNT=2
  STEER_GPU_TYPE=H100`; activate `.venv`; `set -a; source /workspace/.env; set +a`; `export PYTHONPATH=.`.
- **`model_profile.py`** is the registry: MODEL_ID → tag / num_layers / hidden / depth-scaled layer grids.
  `tag=None` for 32B (legacy un-suffixed paths, EXACT old constants); a new model → `_{tag}` artifact paths
  (so it never clobbers the committed 32B results). To add a model: add a profile entry (grids: `s6_layers`
  ⊆ `extract_layers`; scale to depth) — `config.py`/`introspect_config.py`/`s6_config.py` consult it.
- **S7 pipeline order** (each cached; `--assert-cached` verifies): `run_verify.py` (hook 4/4) →
  `run_s7_extract.py` (vectors+norms+proj_std → `concept_vectors_<tag>` + `s7_norms_<tag>`) →
  `run_s7_steerscan.py` → `run_grade_salience.py --gen-file gen_s7_steerscan_<tag>.jsonl` →
  `build_s7_library.py` (→ `concept_library_<tag>.{json,npz}`) → `run_match_layers.py --concepts subset`
  → `run_grade_salience.py --gen-file gen_match_layers_<tag>.jsonl` → `analyze_match_layers.py`
  (→ `matched_points_layers_<tag>.json` + `s6_projstats_<tag>.json`) → `run_s7_introspect.py` (+grade) →
  `run_s6_sweep.py --pairs subset --tag <tag> --layers ... --n-ctx 6` → `run_grade_coherence.py` →
  `analyze_s7.py` → `plot_s7.py` + `inspect_s7.py`.
- **Llama-70B specifics:** load ~10 min first time (140 GB download into the `hf-cache-introspect` volume),
  ~1–2 min after. 30 shards. Concurrency ~12–16 is comfortable on 2×H100.

## Segment-7 gotchas / tribal knowledge
- **`image.env` bakes STEER_MODEL_ID into the container** — without it MODEL_ID silently defaults to Qwen-32B
  inside the container (Modal does not forward local env). Editing `steering_modal.py` (this + the family-
  agnostic `eos_ids` / `_ROLE_RE`) refreshed all Modal caches via `code_hash`; 32B results were committed and
  NOT re-run. For Qwen the eos set is byte-identical to before ({<|im_end|>}); Llama adds
  {<|eot_id|>,<|end_of_text|>,<|eom_id|>}.
- **Llama HAS a first-token activation sink** (ratio 7–309×) → median-norm strength unit transfers.
- **Llama garbles into REPETITION at alpha_rel≥2** (coherent window ~0.5–1.5; presence-2 WORKING
  out-of-support) → lead the SUB latent; `build_s7_library` clips the cal-grid anchor to [0.5,2.0] so the
  calibration explores the coherent low-alpha window. The latent probe-mass stays HIGH even at working
  (0.82–0.99), so the working latent is usable on Llama (unlike 32B's working-latent collapse).
- **Llama diff-of-means directions are LESS orthogonal than 32B** (mean |cos| ~0.34 @L24; abstract/style
  concepts freedom/time/all_caps/mountain nearly collinear, |cos| 0.84–0.97). `analyze_s7` re-runs the
  decisive metrics on the near-orthogonal pairs (|cos|<0.5) — the source negative + B1 peak both hold.
- **The on-manifold patch carries presence only at L0–L24 on Llama** (collapses L32+; earlier than 32B's
  L48). So the B5 patch arm is meaningful at L≤24; the cleanest anomaly point is L8 (patch presence 2.17,
  read "prompt" 0.17) vs the synthetic (read "injection" 0.94).
- **`run_grade_salience` falls back to `data/concepts.json` descs** when the library doesn't exist yet (so it
  can grade the steer scan before `build_s7_library`).
- **`s5_config` partition assert is guarded** (skips when the library has <all-46 concepts) so a minimal
  library imports cleanly. `build_s7_library` tags all 16 subset concepts `robust`; `non_discriminable_near_
  neighbors=[]` (cross-category subset).
- **The reused `analyze_s6/plot_s6/inspect_s6` are 32B-only** (hardcoded paths); S7 uses dedicated
  `analyze_s7/plot_s7/inspect_s7` that read the tagged battery files + the validated scoring formulas.
- **Qwen2.5-72B steers far more readily than Llama-70B** (coherent presence-2 WORKING in support L16-L56;
  near-orthogonal directions like 32B; comprehension 1.0). Llama is the harder case (narrow coherent window;
  less-orthogonal directions). Both give the same source NEGATIVE.
- **inspect_s7 had a label-comparison bug** (correct_label loads as float 1.0; compare via `lbl()` = the
  '1'/'2' string, as analyze_s7 does with `as_lbl`). Fixed.
- **plot_s7 derives the model display name from MODEL_ID** (`MNAME`); the 32B overlay is always the
  comparison baseline.

## Segment-4 gotchas / tribal knowledge
- **Controlled 2AFC MUST use neutral digit labels** (1/2), NOT concept words: injecting concept A's
  vector mechanically boosts the "A" token logit → concept-word answers are a direct-logit artifact.
- **Strong "answer-1" position bias** in the 2AFC → ALWAYS pool over both counterbalanced orders.
- **On-manifold patch presence depends on the meta:** neutral continuation → presence≈1.7 coherent;
  detection-meta + primed → the model treats the patch as conversation (denies injection) → presence≈0.
  The latter is the anomaly finding, not a bug.

## Segment-3 gotchas / tribal knowledge
- **NO `steering_modal.py` edits were needed** in S3 — every battery passes different vectors / meta-
  questions / a combined (concept+bias) vector to the EXISTING `introspect_generate` /
  `introspect_logits` / `projection_stats` / `rmsnorm_delta` / `extract_concept`. This deliberately
  preserved all S2 Modal caches. **Keep it that way** unless a new GPU primitive is truly required.
- **Multi-axis matching:** matched-projz alpha = z_concept × proj_std(pert dir); matched-norm =
  concept alpha. At matched-projz, random/shuffled land at post-RMSNorm Δ ≈ 0.6 (concept 0.77) and at
  matched-norm ≈ 0.99 — both give ~0 detection, so the conclusion is robust to the internal axis.
- **projection-z ≠ functional impact:** a random vector at 50σ is coherent + undetected; the concept at
  50σ biases output. So Segment-4 salience matching MUST use output-presence, not internal axes alone.
- **Forced 2AFC needs the diff-in-diff vs the random control** (per-option preference bias inflates raw
  accuracy). **Detection-bias vector** must be judged vs no-injection+bias and random+bias (it is pure
  yes-bias here). **Combined-vector trick:** inject V by passing vector=V, alpha_abs=||V|| (the hook
  unit-normalizes), so concept*a_c + detect_dir*a_b is one additive injection at one layer.

- **Segment-2 pipeline (each cached; add `--assert-cached` to verify no new work):**

## Gotchas / tribal knowledge
- **Position scope:** `context_only` injects only on the unrelated-context content tokens (verified:
  `introspect_generate` returns the decoded `steered_span`). It barely propagates to the answer
  (presence 0.29 at working vs 2.44 for `all`) — a real "residue washes out" finding, NOT a bug.
- **Primary detection metric = CLEAR detection (`detection==2`)**; `det≥1` is reported as the
  inclusive secondary (it can include hedged "explicit-denial + residual-affect" cases the grader
  scores 1). **Headline identification = `id_joint2_cluster` = (`detection==2` AND `cluster_match`)**,
  and report **P(correct id | clear detection)** (~0.73 L24 / 0.81 matched). The standalone id grader
  over-credits emotion/topic *expression* without injection-framing — don't use it as the headline.
- **Sub-threshold is reported honestly as "tiny + misidentified", not 0:** `analyze_introspect.py`
  surfaces the empirically-sub-threshold detections (8/2375 = 0.34% det≥1, 12% id-correct) and the
  a-priori collider-free regime view; the data inspection has a "SUB-THRESHOLD DETECTIONS" group.
- **Sub-threshold is EMPIRICAL** (graded output-presence < 1.5), not the strength regime label — the
  output-bias onset varies by (concept, layer), so some "sub" regime cells are actually biased.
- **Logit-lens:** control P("No")≈1 so P(Yes)≈0 baseline; the signal is P(Yes) rising under
  injection. `pirate` has an affirmative-style confound (→"Yes/Aye"); excluded from the aggregate.
  Concept-token probe is mechanical (introspect≈neutral) — never headline it as latent id.
- **DON'T `pkill` grading runs.** `llm.py` now uses a 180 s per-call stream timeout so hung Anthropic
  streams retry quickly; a previous pkill (old 60-min timeout) lost ~$197 of cost tracking (SIGTERM
  skips atexit) — a labelled RECONCILIATION entry was added to `total_cost.jsonl`.
- **Cold-start safetensors race:** at high concurrency several containers can hit a transient
  safetensors load error on simultaneous cold-start; Modal reschedules those calls to healthy
  containers, so output is complete (just a few wasted cold starts). Concurrency 6 is comfortable.
- `total_cost.jsonl` is force-committed; `*.npz` and `file_cache_dir/` are git-ignored.

## Library / data the protocol consumes (from S1)
- `results/concept_library.json` (manifest) + `concept_library.npz` (committed float16 UNIT vectors
  `"{concept}|L{layer}"`). Inject `working.alpha_abs × unit` via the pre-hook on `layers[L]`. Use the
  per-concept `tier` (46 robust / 4 borderline / 1 fail=secrecy); `summary.non_discriminable_near_
  neighbors` drives cluster-lenient identification. `norm_reference_median_by_layer` → `alpha_abs`.

## Current state
Segment 2 complete: validated detection+identification graders, full layer×strength×priming×position
sweep + full-robust confirmation + controls + altmeta + logit-lens, all cached & `--assert-cached`-
reproducible, data inspection + reviews done. Headline in `write_up.md` / `write_up_s2_*`.

# Discrimination-benchmark design doc (Segment 4) — prompt-vs-activation source-attribution

**Status:** finalized design + pre-registered kill criterion for the Segment-5 planner to consume.
Self-contained. Authored phase `s4_benchmark_design_pilot`. Governed by the Segment-3 gate
(`results/replication_gate.md`, PARTIAL) and the OVERALL_PLAN §1 caveats. Pilot results that test
this design are in `write_up_s4_benchmark_design_pilot.md` (don't re-derive them here).

> **PILOT OUTCOME (resolves the §2b tension; full results in the phase write-up).** The 2AFC channel
> is VALID for format/knowable-source (positive-control reads a knowable source at ceiling;
> comprehension 1.0), but it is NOT a validated source-detector. The controlled 2AFC is **above
> chance at the matched sub-threshold point** (latent 0.77, ΔP +0.44) — a genuine readout of *which of
> two co-present concepts is internally more active* (a bounded positive: introspection of relative
> activation magnitude). **But three de-confounded controls show this is NOT source-attribution**:
> (K3 salience, FU1) when the prompt *emphasizes the non-injected* concept the model follows the
> prompt and picks the injected concept only **0.10** (latent; sampled 0.05) → relative-salience,
> prompt-dominated; (K2 anomaly, FU2) the *explicit* "prompt or injection?" question attributes the
> **off-manifold synthetic to "injection" (1.00, both scopes)** but the **on-manifold patch to
> "prompt" (~0.55)**; (FU3) yet the which-of-two 2AFC discriminates that SAME on-manifold patch even
> *more* strongly (latent **0.94** > synthetic 0.77) — so the 2AFC "which is injected" is a salience
> readout that **labels the patch 'injected' while the explicit question calls it 'prompt'** (an
> internal inconsistency proving it isn't source-access). **Outcome bucket = K2 + K3.** The controls
> unmasked a confounded positive — the segment's core value. Segment 5: run the benchmark as a
> *bounded salience/anomaly instrument*, lead with the de-confounded FU1/FU2/FU3 battery, stay on 32B.
> See §6 and the phase write-up.

---

## 0. The scientific question this benchmark must answer

**Can Qwen2.5-32B distinguish a concept being *injected into its activations* from the same concept
merely being *present in its prompt*?** A reliable, fair, above-chance discrimination — that is NOT
explained by salience mismatch, anomaly detection, output-reading, or prompt-inference — would be
evidence for **introspective access to the source/nature of an internal state**. The whole design
exists to make a positive result mean that, and to make a null informative.

**What we already know (governs every choice below):**
- **Output-reading is confirmed, not just "consistent with".** S3 + the S4 output-decoupling test
  (§3 of INSTRUCTIONS; `analyze_decouple.py`) show 32B's introspective detection is **gated by
  realized downstream output-presence**: holding internal magnitude fixed at working strength and
  forcing the concept's output appearance low (token suppression) drives clear-detection to **0**
  (presence≤1: 0/14); within-concept (pirate) detection falls 1.00→0.50 as presence 3.0→1.38. So
  any discrimination that rides on output-presence is output-reading, not source-attribution.
- **projection-z ≠ functional impact.** A 50σ random vector is coherent and undetected while the
  concept direction biases output. ⇒ salience matching must be **load-bearing on OUTPUT-PRESENCE**,
  not internal off-manifoldness.
- **The on-manifold patch IS coherent and output-present — but the model treats it as
  *conversational*, not injected** (matched-salience characterization, `run_salience.py`): grafting
  REAL concept-context residuals onto the context tokens (`patch_introspect_generate`,
  `context_only`, β=1) yields output **presence ≈ 1.7, coherence 3.0** under a neutral continuation
  (e.g. cat-patch → "It sounds like the kitten was really focused on its playtime!"). So the earlier
  smoke-test "washout to 0" was an artifact of the detection-meta + primed system, under which the
  model **denies injection and attributes the concept to "our conversation."** This is the key
  finding: in the **explicit** source/detection framing the model **flags the OFF-manifold synthetic
  as injected but treats the ON-manifold patch as ordinary conversational content** — the signature
  of **anomaly/context detection, not source-attribution** (pilot FU2). *Crucial nuance (pilot FU3):*
  the **which-of-two 2AFC nonetheless discriminates the patched concept** (it's a salience readout),
  so the patch is *labeled "injected" by the 2AFC yet "prompt" by the explicit question* — an internal
  inconsistency that is itself decisive evidence the 2AFC isn't source-attribution. Consequence: a fair
  on-manifold comparison IS available at a working-ish presence (the patch), and the explicit-source +
  2AFC + opposed-emphasis battery (FU1/FU2/FU3) is the deepest control.
- **Spontaneous detection ≈ 0 sub-threshold** (S2/S3) behaviorally, latently, and under forced
  Yes/No. So the benchmark leads with a **forced-choice 2AFC source-attribution** (a constrained,
  potentially more sensitive channel) — but "constrained ⇒ more sensitive" is NOT assumed (S3's
  forced Yes/No was constrained and still failed); we test it (§5b).

**Operational definition of "introspection" (sharpened via the design review).** We require
introspection to mean **access to information about the source of the internal state that is NOT
recoverable from the model's own emitted tokens up to the decision point**. A model that simply runs
its normal forward pass, notices the next-token distribution is disproportionately tilted toward A,
and *deduces* "A must be injected" is doing **forward-pass / output inference, not introspection**.
This is enforced two ways: (a) the **first-token / pre-output latent readout** (read the source
decision at the FIRST answer token, before any concept-biased token is emitted), and (b) **output-
token suppression** during any sampled answer. A signal that survives BOTH is the closest we can get
to source-access not reducible to reading impending output.

**Reframe (design-review-driven, important).** Because on 32B "output-present + coherent +
on-manifold" is empirically empty (§0), the honest construct is **discrimination of two causal
sources of a concept's elevated influence — an *injected* channel vs a *textual* channel** — not
"introspection of on-manifold residual content." A central, *positive* scientific deliverable of the
benchmark is therefore to **demonstrate the on-manifold washout**: if discrimination exists only for
the off-manifold synthetic vector and collapses to chance for the on-manifold patch, the rigorous
conclusion is "32B has no source-introspection of on-manifold concept activations; it can at most
detect off-manifold geometric anomalies." A working-strength positive must be framed as
anomaly/output-reading, never as introspection (both reviewers call an unqualified positive a
systematic over-claim).

---

## 1. The two routes (benchmark conditions)

For a target concept X on a trial, X reaches the model by **exactly one route** (or neither). All
routes share the SAME unrelated context, the SAME meta-question, the SAME layer L, and the SAME
injected token positions — the ONLY difference is the route.

| Route | How X enters | Manifold status | In input text? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Prompt** | X is mentioned in / is the topic of the unrelated context text | on-manifold (contextualized) | **yes** |
| **Activation–synthetic** | S1 diff-of-means unit vector for X, `α·v_X`, injected at residual point L | **off-manifold** (static offset; ~40–67σ at working) | no |
| **Activation–on-manifold (patch)** | REAL residual activations captured from a forward pass on concept-bearing donor prompts, patched (replace/blend) into the context at L | **on-manifold** (genuine activations) | no |
| **Baseline–content-free** | random Gaussian / shuffled-concept unit vector at L (matched axis) | off-manifold, no concept | no |
| **Baseline–no-injection** | nothing | on-manifold | no |

**Positions / layer / scope.** Primary L = **24** (mid-stack; S3 detection peak, library steering
band). Injection lands on the **unrelated-context content tokens** (the `context_only` mask, via the
fast-tokenizer offset map) and, for the working operating point of the synthetic route, on **all
positions** (`position=all`, the S2/S3 protocol). The prompt route puts X in those same context
tokens as text. The on-manifold patch can only sensibly target context tokens (replacing the
meta/answer residuals with concept activations would destroy the question) — documented asymmetry.

**Context construction.** Reuse the 15 audited concept-agnostic S2 contexts (`introspect_config.CONTEXTS`)
for the injection/baseline routes. For the **prompt route**, the concept is woven into the context
(see §1a). For the **controlled 2AFC** (§5), the context mentions **two** concepts symmetrically.

### 1a. Prompt-route construction (matched to the injection's "unrelated context" framing)
The prompt route must put X in the input *without* turning the trial into a different task. Two
intensities (the prompt-route salience knob, §6):
- **light:** one neutral mention — `"{context} (Earlier we were talking about {X}.)"`.
- **strong:** the context is *about* X — a sentence or two on the topic of X.
A **paraphrase** variant (X present semantically, the literal word absent) is used to measure the
literal-vs-semantic prompt-reading split (§5c).

---

## 2. Discrimination protocols

### 2a. PRIMARY — forced-choice 2AFC source-attribution (two variants)

**(i) Positive-control 2AFC ("source is knowable") — instrument validation, NOT the headline.**
X arrives via prompt-only (literal) OR injection-only (X not in the text). The model is told X is
active and must classify the source:
> *"The concept of **{X}** is active in your processing right now. Did it become active because it
> was in our conversation / your prompt, or because it was injected directly into your internal
> activations (separate from the text)? Answer with one word: **prompt** or **injection**."*
Because X is literally in the input on prompt trials and absent on injection trials, this is
**trivially solvable by re-reading the prompt**. Its job is to **validate the instrument**: if the
model can't read a *knowable* source here, a null elsewhere is uninformative (this is the test the
S3 2AFC failed). It also **measures the prompt-reading strategy** (literal vs paraphrase, §5c).

**(ii) Controlled 2AFC ("which concept is injected") — THE HEADLINE, re-reading neutralized.**
**Both** concepts A and B are mentioned **symmetrically** (one mention each, counterbalanced order)
in the unrelated context, so both are literally in the input. Exactly one of {A, B} is **also**
injected into the activations (or neither, for the floor). The model must say which:
> *"Earlier in our conversation, both **{A}** and **{B}** came up. Right now, one of those two
> concepts is *also* being injected directly into your internal activations (separate from the
> text) — or neither is. Which concept is being injected into your activations: **{A}** or **{B}**?
> Answer with only the one word."*
- **Chance = 50%.** Literal re-reading is **useless** (both A and B are in the text). To answer
  above chance the model must use INTERNAL information — that one concept's activation is boosted
  beyond what the symmetric text explains. This is genuine source-attribution.
- **CRITICAL — neutral digit labels, NOT concept words (design review, GPT).** The answer must be a
  neutral label `(1)/(2)`, never the concept word: injecting concept A's diff-of-means vector
  **mechanically boosts the "A" token logit**, so a concept-word answer would be a direct
  logit-bias artifact (the same output-channel mechanism), not source-attribution. The digit is
  decoupled from the concept tokens; the option→digit mapping is **counterbalanced** (A is "1" in
  half the trials, "2" in the other half). The first-token latent readout probes P("1") vs P("2").
- **Paired same-prompt estimand.** On the identical context, run inject-A vs inject-B; accuracy over
  the pair cancels common-mode prompt evidence. Counterbalance A/B order and which-is-injected;
  report accuracy net of per-label / answer-token priors, and over the per-item no-injection bias.

### 2b. The §5 sensitivity tension (reasoned explicitly — do NOT assume "constrained ⇒ sensitive")
The controlled 2AFC is the option-(a) framing ("is X *also* injected on top of the prompt?"), which
INSTRUCTIONS warn "risks collapsing into S3's already-failed forced-detection." Honest analysis:
- **Why it might still be more sensitive than forced Yes/No detection:** it is a *relative* (which-
  of-two) judgment, not an absolute one. It does **not require an absolute detection threshold to be
  crossed** — only that the injected concept's internal trace is *marginally more* salient than the
  non-injected concept's. A 2-way comparison of two co-present concepts can read out a sub-threshold
  bias that a yes/no "did anything cross threshold?" cannot. This is a genuine signal forced
  detection lacks.
- **Why it might collapse to S3's failed detection:** both A and B already have prompt-derived
  activations, so injecting A adds a *small extra* component on top of a large prompt-A baseline —
  the prompt **masks** the injection's salience, exactly as the warning says. If the extra injected
  component is below the relative-comparison's noise floor, accuracy → 50%.
- **Adjudication is empirical (the pilot's job).** Pre-registered reading: if the controlled 2AFC is
  at chance at the matched **sub-threshold** operating point (the likely outcome given S2/S3), report
  it as: *the more-sensitive-looking instrument, once the trivial discriminator is controlled,
  reduces to S3's failed detection task* — a **design finding and a kill/pivot trigger (§7)**, not a
  buried null. If it is **above chance sub-threshold AND above the content-free/on-manifold floors**,
  that is the genuine source-attribution signal the project seeks.
- **EMPIRICAL RESOLUTION (pilot).** The controlled 2AFC IS above chance sub-threshold (latent 0.77),
  so it is genuinely *more sensitive than S3's failed forced-detection* — the relative which-of-two
  framing reads a sub-threshold internal salience asymmetry that absolute detection cannot. **But it
  is NOT source-attribution:** the above-chance signal is the model picking *which co-present concept
  is internally more salient*, which (a) is dominated by the prompt when prompt-salience opposes the
  injection (FU1: picks injected only **0.10** under opposed emphasis), and (b) discriminates the
  on-manifold patch just as well/better (FU3: latent **0.94** > synthetic 0.77) while the model's
  *explicit* source judgment of that same patch is "prompt" (FU2) — an internal inconsistency. So the
  which-of-two readout measures **relative concept salience**, not "this concept came from injection
  vs prompt." The §2b worry resolves to: *more sensitive than detection, yes — but the added
  sensitivity is salience discrimination, not source access.*

### 2c. SECONDARY — detection-asymmetry (the spontaneous channel, expected weak)
Reuse the S2 detection protocol ("do you detect an injected thought?"). Genuine source-attribution ⇒
"injected-thought" rate HIGH under the **activation route** but LOW under the **prompt route** (the
model should know a prompt concept is just input). Caveat: S2/S3 show spontaneous detection ≈0
sub-threshold, so expect this to be a weak instrument; reported, not headlined.

### 2d. PRIMARY output-reading-severed channel — first-token / pre-output latent 2AFC readout
Read the source decision at the **FIRST answer token**, before any concept-biased token is emitted:
for the controlled 2AFC, read P("{A}") vs P("{B}") at the answer position via the logit-lens
(`introspect_logits` / `patch_introspect_logits`, probe_words = the two concept words). The
**graded** readout (ΔlogP(A vs B), more bits/trial than a hard choice) at t+1 has had **no chance to
read concept-biased output** (the model has emitted nothing yet), so it is the cleanest available
attack on the output-reading confound (per both reviewers' "early-decision readout"). Still
imperfect — the pre-first-token state already encodes the impending output — so combine with output
suppression; but a controlled-2AFC signal that appears ONLY in sampled text and NOT in this
first-token readout is output-reading, and a signal present in BOTH is the strongest source-access
evidence the harness can produce. (Behavioral sampled 2AFC and detection-asymmetry remain as the
verbalized channels.)

---

## 3. The trivial-prompt-reading control (a first-class design problem)

Discrimination by **re-reading the input** is *prompt inference, not introspective access*. Handling:
1. **Literal re-reading — controlled by construction (controlled 2AFC, §2a-ii):** both concepts are
   literally in the input, so re-reading cannot pick the injected one. The positive-control 2AFC
   (§2a-i) deliberately leaves it solvable, to (a) validate the instrument and (b) **measure** the
   prompt-reading ceiling.
2. **Measure prompt-reading directly (paraphrase test):** in the positive-control 2AFC, compare
   literal-prompt vs paraphrase-prompt accuracy. A collapse under paraphrase quantifies how much the
   model relies on the literal token. Report controlled-2AFC discrimination **net of** this.
3. **Two levels of inference — state the bound plainly.** *Literal* re-reading (token present) is
   controlled by §3.1. *Semantic / probabilistic* inference (the model reasoning "my input is *about*
   A, so A probably came from my prompt") is subtler. The controlled 2AFC neutralizes the **symmetric**
   part (A and B equally mentioned, so "which is more prompt-like" is balanced), but **cannot fully
   exclude** the model reasoning about asymmetric textual salience or doing probabilistic
   source-inference. **Therefore: ruling out literal re-reading does NOT establish *direct
   introspective access* over *semantic inference*** (the deep "Dissociating Direct Access from
   Inference" concern). Even a clean positive controlled-2AFC result is bounded as "source-
   attribution not explained by literal re-reading, salience, anomaly, or output-reading" — NOT
   "proven direct (non-inferential) introspection."

---

## 4. MANDATORY anomaly controls (per the S3 gate)

- **Content-free perturbation baseline.** Run the controlled 2AFC with a **random/shuffled** vector
  injected instead of a concept vector (neither A nor B is actually injected). The model is still
  forced to pick A or B. Expected accuracy = **50%** (no concept is boosted). If the model instead
  "discriminates" a content-free injection from prompt-presence as well as a concept injection (in
  the positive-control framing), the benchmark measures **perturbation detection, not source-
  attribution** → kill (§7). *Matching note (reviewer):* the ideal content-free control matches the
  concept arm on projection-z AND functional impact. We match projection-z (S3 machinery). **Matched
  functional impact is intrinsically unavailable for a content-free vector** — functional impact IS
  concept-specificity (a random vector with concept-level functional impact would not be content-
  free) — so the content-free arm is necessarily lower-impact; the on-manifold patch is the control
  that holds concept-content while removing off-manifoldness.
- **Naturalistic / on-manifold (activation-patched) injection.** Run the controlled 2AFC with the
  **on-manifold patch** of concept A (or B) instead of the synthetic vector. If discrimination works
  for the off-manifold synthetic but **vanishes for the on-manifold patch**, the apparent signal was
  **anomaly detection**, not source-attribution. **Pilot scope note (empirical, §0):** the patch can
  only be matched at **sub-threshold** (it cannot reach a coherent output-present state), so the
  on-manifold control is run at the sub-threshold operating point — which is the regime of interest
  anyway. Full Segment-5 patch spec in §8.

---

## 5. Multi-axis matched-salience procedure (THE CRUX)

The result can be trivially confounded by **salience mismatch**. Match the prompt and activation
routes on **multiple axes** and **report how the headline discrimination moves with the matching
metric** — never pick one metric and stop.

- **Axis 1 (LOAD-BEARING) — downstream output concept-presence.** Tune the prompt-route mention
  intensity and the activation-route strength so they produce **matched graded output-presence** on
  free-text generations (S1 `grade_output`, 0–3). Primary because S3+S4 prove detection rides on
  output-presence. Report the headline 2AFC accuracy **at matched output-presence**, primarily at the
  **low / sub-threshold** matched point (where output-reading can't drive the result).
- **Axis 2 — output COHERENCE.** Match graded coherence (S1 `grade_output`) so "my output feels
  broken ⇒ injection" can't be the cue. (The synthetic route can degrade coherence at strength; the
  on-manifold patch + matching close this.)
- **Axis 3 — logit-level target-token logprob lift / next-token KL** at a readout point.
- **Axis 4 — internal projection / "concept-present" readout** at a downstream layer.

**Procedure:** (1) sweep each route's strength knob; (2) grade free-text output-presence + coherence;
(3) pick, per concept, the prompt-mention intensity and the injection strength that land on a common
**low (sub-threshold)** output-presence target and (separately) a **working** target; (4) run the
2AFC at those matched points; (5) report 2AFC accuracy as a function of the matched output-presence,
plus its sensitivity when Axes 3–4 are (un)matched. **If matching Axis 1 forces a gross mismatch on
another axis that itself could drive discrimination, that is a core finding (and possibly a kill
condition, §7).**

**Collider warning (design review, GPT/Claude):** match by **strength knob / by design**, NOT by
*selecting trials on realized output-presence* — conditioning the headline on realized drift is
collider selection. We characterize realized presence per route (descriptive, `run_salience.py`) and
run the 2AFC at fixed injection strengths; the **first-token latent readout** (decision before any
output token) is the principled way to sever output-presence without selection. The S4 matched-
salience characterization shows the on-manifold patch reaches presence≈1.7 coherently, so a fair
on-manifold comparison IS available — the patch is treated as conversation, not injection (the
anomaly signature).

---

## 6. Pre-registered KILL / DECISION criterion + what each outcome licenses

Committed **before** the pilot (timestamp + git hash in `progress_log_s4_benchmark_design_pilot.md`).
Operating point for all gates below = the **matched sub-threshold output-presence** point unless
noted; CIs = Wilson 95% (and concept-clustered where n_concepts is small); chance = 50%; content-free
/ no-injection floor estimated empirically.

**Failure modes → pivots:**
- **K1 — Intractable matched comparison.** If output-presence cannot be matched across the routes at
  any usable operating point without a gross mismatch on coherence / logit / internal that itself
  drives discrimination → the fair comparison is intractable. *Pivot:* restrict the headline to the
  sub-threshold residue regime where routes are matchable (the S4 finding already forces the
  on-manifold arm here); if even that is unmatchable, report the benchmark as **not constructible as
  a fair output-matched instrument on 32B** and hand Segment 5 the negative-construction result.
- **K2 — Anomaly-only.** If controlled-2AFC accuracy for the **content-free** perturbation ≈ accuracy
  for the concept injection, OR concept discrimination **vanishes for the on-manifold patch** while
  surviving only for the off-manifold synthetic → the benchmark measures **anomaly detection, not
  source-attribution**. *Pivot:* report as an anomaly-detection instrument; do not run the full
  source-attribution benchmark at scale; lean on the on-manifold patch as the primary arm in Seg 5.
- **K3 — Trivial / prompt-reading.** If the controlled-2AFC signal collapses when the literal token
  is removed (paraphrase) or is fully accounted for by the positive-control prompt-reading ceiling →
  prompt inference, not introspection. *Pivot:* redesign toward harder re-reading control (concept
  outside re-readable context) or report as prompt-inference.
- **K4 — ≈Chance / output-dependent (the S2/S3-predicted outcome).** If controlled-2AFC accuracy is
  at chance at the matched **sub-threshold** point, with any above-chance signal confined to the
  output-biasing (working) strength → consistent with output-reading, no introspection. *Pivot:*
  report a clean, fair **negative**; proceed to Segment 5 with the benchmark as a **bounded** null
  instrument; emphasize the latent/forced channels; **do NOT escalate model on this alone** (default
  per S3 stay-on-32B) unless §7 shows the pilot is **capability-limited, not confound-limited**.
- **K5 — Instrument invalid.** If the **positive-control 2AFC** is at chance (the model can't read a
  *knowable* source) → the 2AFC instrument is invalid (as the S3 2AFC was) and any controlled-2AFC
  null is uninformative. *Pivot:* fix the instrument (phrasing, parsing, priming) before any headline.

**What a POSITIVE controlled-2AFC result licenses — ONLY if ALL hold:**
(i) survives **output-presence (and coherence) matching** (Axis 1–2); (ii) **exceeds the content-free
baseline** (K2); (iii) **holds for the on-manifold patch** (K2); (iv) appears at the **sub-threshold**
matched point (K4); (v) is **not explained by prompt-inference** — survives paraphrase and exceeds the
positive-control prompt-reading ceiling (K3). Then it licenses: *"32B has source-attribution
introspection not reducible to salience/anomaly/output-reading/literal-inference"* — **bounded** by §3
(does not establish direct access over *semantic* inference). Absent any of (i)–(v), the positive
reduces to that missing confound and must be framed as such.

> **CRITICAL for the Segment-5 planner (pilot-confirmed): the controlled-2AFC discrimination is a
> relative-SALIENCE readout, not source-attribution — so a 2AFC "positive" does NOT license an
> introspection claim.** Leg (iii) (does the 2AFC discrimination hold for the on-manifold patch?) is
> directly tested in FU3 and is **SATISFIED** — the 2AFC discriminates the on-manifold patch *more*
> strongly (latent 0.94) than the off-manifold synthetic (0.77). That sounds like passing leg (iii),
> but it actually *disproves* the source interpretation: the 2AFC labels the patch "injected" while
> the model's *explicit* source question calls the same patch "prompt" (FU2). The which-of-two signal
> tracks **which concept is internally more active**, regardless of true source, and is dominated by
> the prompt when prompt-salience opposes it (FU1: 0.10). **So the pilot found NO instrument that
> isolates source-attribution from salience/anomaly/prompt-dominance.** Segment 5 should NOT chase a
> 2AFC "positive" as introspection; its value is to *characterize* the salience/anomaly instrument and
> quantify the prompt-dominance + manifold-gating, leading with the de-confounded FU1/FU2/FU3 battery.
> (A genuine source-attribution positive would require an instrument the pilot did not find — e.g. one
> that dissociates "more active" from "came from injection"; this is a Segment-5+ design problem, not a
> target to assume reachable.)

**Default expectation (honest):** given S2/S3 + the S4 output-decoupling result, the **most likely
outcome is K4** (≈chance sub-threshold; any working-strength signal is output-reading). That is a
legitimate, valuable result if the design is fair (matched salience + anomaly controls + trivial-
discriminator control) and the instrument is validated (K5 passes).

---

## 7. Recommended Segment-5 configuration
- **Model:** Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (stay; revisit escalation only if the pilot is provably capability-
  limited, not confound-limited).
- **Headline instruments (pilot-revised):** the **de-confounded FU1 (salience-vs-source, emphasis
  counterbalanced across label positions)** and **FU2 (anomaly, scope-matched synthetic-vs-patch)** are
  the *primary* arms — they carry the interpretation. The controlled 2AFC ("which of two co-present
  concepts is injected") at the matched sub-threshold point is reported as a *salience* readout (its
  above-chance sub signal is relative-salience, prompt-dominated, not source), with the positive-control
  2AFC for instrument/format validation and the paraphrase prompt-reading measurement. **Do NOT scale
  the raw controlled-2AFC accuracy as if it were source-attribution.** (The pilot's first-pass FU1/FU2
  had a position-bias / scope confound; the counterbalanced + scope-matched versions are the ones to
  run — see `analyze_followup.py`.)
- **Arms (all mandatory):** prompt, activation-synthetic, activation-on-manifold-patch (sub-threshold),
  content-free, no-injection.
- **Concepts:** full robust tier (exclude `secrecy`); use category-balanced concept *pairs* with
  matched near-neighbor distance (avoid pairing non-discriminable near-neighbors — emotions cluster).
- **Layer:** L24 headline; the layer sweep is Segment 6 (incl. the layer-0 sanity check where the two
  routes should coincide).
- **Salience matching:** load-bearing on output-presence; report accuracy vs matched presence + Axis
  3–4 sensitivity. **Full on-manifold patch spec:** donors = length-matched concept-bearing prompts;
  patch the context content tokens at L (replace/blend β; β=1 = exact on-manifold); report achieved
  output-presence/coherence; the working operating point is **not** available on-manifold (S4 finding)
  — so the on-manifold arm anchors the sub-threshold comparison.
- **Stats:** n≥ the S3 per-cell (concepts × contexts × orders), Wilson + concept-clustered CIs, 50%
  chance baseline, content-free/no-injection floors; fixed seeds; everything cached + `--assert-cached`.
- **Required confound controls (reviewer-driven):** (a) **counterbalance** A/B option order AND
  which-is-injected, and report accuracy net of per-option / answer-token priors (score diff-in-diff
  vs the random control as in S3); (b) **per-concept (held-out) generalization** — never headline a
  pooled number that a few token-embedding-aligned concepts could carry; require the effect to hold
  on concepts not used to tune phrasing; (c) **task-comprehension sanity check** — a non-injection
  "which concept did I emphasize more in the prompt?" control confirms the model understands the
  2AFC frame (else a null is uninformative); (d) **coherence-gate trials** independently of the
  concept axis (drop degenerate generations before scoring the verbalized 2AFC); (e) match the
  **full output-presence distribution** (or condition trial-wise), not just the mean.

---

## 8. Judgment-heavy choices (documented; reviewed via query_all_models + research-reviewer)
- **Controlled 2AFC = "which of two co-present concepts is injected"** (vs plain "is X also injected"
  detection): chosen because it neutralizes literal re-reading AND is a *relative* judgment that may
  read sub-threshold bias an absolute detector can't — but its sensitivity is NOT assumed; the pilot
  adjudicates the §2b tension.
- **On-manifold = real-residual patch** (vs calibrated-low-σ synthetic): the patch grafts genuine
  donor-concept activations (coherent + presence≈1.7 via context-graft). **Limitation (reviewer):** the
  pilot patch cycles donor residuals *positionally* (`D[t % P]`) — each context position gets an
  individually-on-manifold real residual, but it is a *bag of scrambled-position real residuals*, not a
  coherent on-manifold *sequence* aligned to the actual context tokens; "β=1 = exact on-manifold" is
  true per-vector, not per-sequence. Segment 5 should patch **length-matched donors position-for-
  position**. A calibrated-low-σ synthetic is a secondary projection-on-manifold variant.
- **Matched-salience load-bearing on output-presence** (not internal axes): forced by S3's
  projection-z ≠ functional-impact finding and the S4 output-decoupling result.
- **Sub-threshold is the headline regime** (not working): working-strength discrimination is
  confounded by output-reading (S4 §3); the project cares about pre-output introspection.
- **Kill thresholds pre-registered** (§6) before the pilot to avoid post-hoc rationalization.
