# Replication success gate + Segment-4 decision — phase `s3_confounds_validity`

Self-contained artifact for the Segment-4 planner. **Part A (the gate) was pre-registered BEFORE any
Segment-3 confound result was generated or examined** (timestamp 05/30/2026 12:20, commit 86bd78a; see
`writeups/progress_log_s3_confounds_validity.md`). Part B (evaluation) and Part C (Segment-4
recommendation) are filled in AFTER the experiments.

---

## Part A — Pre-registered gate (committed 05/30/2026 12:20, commit 86bd78a)

**Scientific question:** does Qwen2.5-32B have a genuine **concept-specific, output-independent**
introspection signal, or is its apparent "detection" explained by **generic anomaly/perturbation
detection** and/or **reading its own already-biased output**?

**Fixed choices (pre-registered):**
- Primary detection metric = **CLEAR detection** (LLM-grader `detection==2`, reads ONLY the answer,
  explicitly does NOT credit output drift). Secondary = `det≥1`. (Reuses the S2-validated grader.)
- Primary elicitation = **primed** (priming "C") + `position=all`. (Best S2 config.)
- FPR floor = no-injection clear-detection rate (S2: 0/120 ≈ 0; re-confirmed in an identical-config
  arm here).
- Subset = the S2 category-spanning **SUBSET** (cat, ocean, fire, bridge, freedom, time, chaos, fear,
  joy, anger, pirate, all_caps). Operating points = **sub-threshold + working**. Layers = **L24**
  (mid-stack detection peak) + L16, with L8 / L40 for the layer-span.
- CIs = Wilson 95%. n per aggregated cell ≥ ~70 (concepts × contexts).

**Multi-axis matching (the crux; reported across axes, never cherry-picked):**
"Concept-specificity established at axis X" ≝ **concept clear-detection CI-lower > perturbation
clear-detection CI-upper (non-overlapping 95% CIs) AND concept rate ≥ 2× perturbation rate.** Axes:
- (a) matched **raw norm** (naive baseline),
- (b1) matched **projection-z** — PRIMARY internal / anomaly-magnitude axis (σ off the natural
  projection distribution; the most direct operationalization of "matched anomaly magnitude"),
- (b2) matched **post-RMSNorm Δ** — secondary internal axis (reported as achieved values),
- (c) matched **output-coherence** — output axis (S1 coherence grader; controls for random-vector
  output-garbling).
Perturbations: matched-norm **random Gaussian**, **shuffled** concept vector, **other-concept** vector
(concept-presence matching applies only to other-concept; random/shuffled match on coherence/internal
axes). Anomaly baseline for the gate = **max** detection over {random, shuffled} at the matched axis.

**Component 1 — Concept-specificity above anomaly (WORKING strength).** PASS-1 iff at working, primed:
concept-specificity is established **at matched projection-z (b1)** AND **at matched output-coherence
(c)**; concept clear-detection CI excludes the FPR floor; FPR ≤ 0.05.

**Component 2 — Identification specificity.** PASS-2 iff, on cells where the model names a concept under
injection, P(named = injected concept | cluster-lenient) ≥ 0.5 AND ≥ 3× the mismatched/random-injection
confabulation baseline, with non-overlapping CIs.

**Component 3 — Output-independence (sub-threshold / output-matched).** PASS-3 iff the Component-1
concept>perturbation ordering (non-overlapping CIs AND ≥2×, matched projection-z) holds at a
**sub-threshold / matched-low-output-bias** operating point AND concept CI excludes FPR. Optional
corroboration: latent logit-lens P(Yes) (concept rise > perturbation rise at sub-threshold) and the
forced-choice 2AFC channel.

**Elicitation-lever clause.** If free-text detection fails but the **forced-choice 2AFC**
(concept vs *distant* distractor) at sub-threshold beats BOTH 50% chance AND the random-vector control
(non-overlapping CIs), that counts as concept-specific evidence via a constrained channel and can lift
FAIL→PARTIAL / satisfy Component 3 by the constrained channel. A refusal-ablation / detection-bias
boost counts only if it raises concept-specific, sub-threshold detection WITHOUT equally raising FPR
and random-vector "detection" (else it is mere yes-bias).

**Pre-defined outcomes:**
- **PASS** — all 3 components pass. Segment 4 proceeds as planned (anomaly controls recommended;
  headline may be framed as genuine introspection).
- **ANOMALY-ONLY / PARTIAL** — Component 1 and/or 2 pass at WORKING strength but Component 3 fails, OR
  concept-specificity holds at matched-projz but vanishes at matched-coherence (or vice versa). The
  Segment-4 **naturalistic/on-manifold injection + content-free perturbation baseline become
  MANDATORY**; forced-choice / latent channels emphasized; headline framed accordingly.
- **FAIL** — concept detection ≈ perturbation at matched internal off-manifoldness at BOTH working and
  sub-threshold, even after stronger elicitation. Genuine concept-specific introspection ≈ 0 on 32B
  under this paradigm. Do NOT abort: Segment 4 proceeds with the **discrimination benchmark as the
  PRIMARY instrument** (a different, possibly more sensitive forced-choice source-attribution measure),
  anomaly controls mandatory, with an explicit statement of what a positive/negative discrimination
  result licenses; weigh model escalation.

---

## Part B — Evaluation against the gate (05/30/2026, after the §3–§7 batteries)

All numbers below are primed, L24 (primary mid-stack layer; L16 comparable, det2 0.25) unless noted; subset = 12 concepts ×
8 contexts (n=96/cell); CLEAR detection (det==2), Wilson 95% CI. No-injection FPR (det==2) = 0/96 ≈ 0.
Sources: `results/perturb_summary.json`, `timing_summary.json`, `forced_summary.json`,
`perturb_logitlens_summary.json`, `refusal_summary.json`.

**Component 1 — concept-specificity above the content-free anomaly baseline (WORKING).** **PASS.**
- concept @ working: **0.208 [0.139, 0.300]** (L16: 0.250 [0.174, 0.345]).
- content-free perturbation (max of random, shuffled):
  - matched **projection-z** (50.8σ, both): random **0.000 [0,0.038]**, shuffled **0.010 [0.002,0.057]** —
    answers are COHERENT (coh ≈ 3.0), so the ~0 is genuine non-detection, not garbling.
  - matched **output-coherence**: random 0.000, shuffled 0.010 (other-CONCEPT 0.052, but that arm injects
    a real wrong concept, not content-free).
  - matched raw norm (random pushed to 108σ — MORE off-manifold): random 0.000, shuffled 0.042.
  Concept CI-lower (0.139) > perturbation CI-upper (0.057) → non-overlapping; ratio ≥ 4–20×; concept CI
  excludes FPR (0). **Holds at matched projection-z AND matched output-coherence AND matched raw norm.**
  → Detection is **NOT generic (scalar-projection) anomaly detection**: a random/shuffled vector that is
  equally (or more) off-manifold internally yet still coherent is essentially never reported as injected.
  *Honest scope of Component 1 (reviewer-driven):* projection-z is a 1-D "rarity along a direction"
  metric and ≠ functional impact (a 50σ random vector is coherent + undetected), so the cleanest reading
  is that the model is sensitive to *which subspace / functional direction* is perturbed, not that
  "concept beats matched-magnitude anomaly" in a full representation-space sense. A **covariance-matched /
  on-manifold random control** (random direction drawn from the activation distribution / in the active
  subspace) would adjudicate "concept-specific" vs "functional-subspace / output-disruption" detection;
  that control is **on-manifold by construction and therefore Segment-4 scope** — and is **made mandatory**
  by this gate (Part C). Within Segment 3's content-free battery (white-noise + shuffled + other-concept),
  the established claim is the weaker-but-solid one: the working-strength signal is **content/subspace-
  specific, not raw off-manifoldness detection**.
  - **Component 1 is NOT independent of Component 3 (important).** At matched projection-z the concept arm
    has output-presence 2.41 while random/shuffled have ~0.01 by construction — no content-free vector can
    match the concept's output-presence — so the concept>content-free *detection* gap IS the
    output-presence gap that Component 3 names as the driver. Components 1 (and 2) PASS are therefore
    **expected under pure output-reading**; only Component 3 bears on output-independent introspection.
    Read Component 1 narrowly: "detection is not triggered by raw off-manifoldness, and requires a
    functional/output-biasing perturbation" — NOT "concept-specific introspection independent of output."
  - **Concept-clustered CIs (the 96 = 12 concepts × 8 contexts are correlated; detection concentrates in
    persona/emotion/abstract types):** concept@working det2 0.208 has a **concept-clustered 95% CI
    [0.000, 0.438]** (vs per-answer Wilson [0.139,0.300]); concept−(random+shuffled) diff +0.203
    [0.000, 0.432] clustered. The gap is real but **concept-type-concentrated** and only borderline once
    concept-clustering is honored (lower bound touches 0 when the few high-detection concepts resample
    out). `results/perturb_robustness.json`.

**Component 2 — identification specificity.** **PASS** (but also expected under output-reading). When the
concept arm names a concept, P(cluster-correct | named) = **0.75** (n_named=44 over 5 concepts;
concept-clustered 95% CI **[0.524, 0.976]**); the content-free arms essentially never name a real library
concept (named-specific rate 0.018, context phrases like "low-key conversation"). 0.75 ≥ 0.5 and ≫ 3× the
≈0 confabulation baseline, non-overlapping even clustered. → identification is content-specific — but at
working strength the output IS about the concept, so naming it correctly is exactly what output-reading
predicts; Component 2 does not by itself evidence introspection.

**Component 3 — output-independence (sub-threshold / matched-low output bias).** **FAIL.**
- concept @ sub-threshold: **0.000 [0, 0.038]** (= random/shuffled/other @ sub = 0.000 = FPR).
- concept @ output-presence < 1.5 (output not visibly biased): **0.000** (n=215) — vs concept @ working
  all-presence 0.229 (mean presence 2.41). Detection rises monotonically with output-presence
  (presence 0→0.000, 2→0.060, 3→0.344) — i.e. it tracks the model reading its own concept-biased output.
- latent logit-lens: concept @ sub P(Yes) ≈ **0.00** at every layer (vs concept @ working ≈ 0.52).
- forced Yes/No: concept @ sub yes-rate 0.000 (sampled) / P(Yes) 0.000 (latent).
- forced **Yes/No** (a VALIDATED channel — working positive control: sampled 0.39 [lower bound; working
  parse-rate 0.69] / latent P(Yes) 0.49 vs none 0.00): concept @ sub yes-rate **0.000** (sampled) /
  P(Yes) **0.000** (latent) — an informative null.
- forced **2AFC** (concept vs distant distractor), diff-in-diff vs the random control: working
  **+0.087 [-0.073, 0.247]** (unparsed-excluded), sub **+0.026 [-0.097, 0.149]** — CIs span 0. **Caveat (reviewer-driven): the
  2AFC is an insensitive/invalid instrument** — it is at chance even at WORKING strength, where a strong
  identification-specific signal demonstrably exists (free-text P(cluster|named)=0.75), so its sub null is
  weak evidence and the elicitation-lever clause was effectively un-testable via 2AFC. The
  output-independence verdict therefore rests on the validated channels (free-text, latent, forced
  Yes/No), not the 2AFC.
- detection-bias-vector (refusal-ablation, ADDITIVE form): at bias that raises concept detection it raises
  **no-injection (FPR) and random "detection" EQUALLY** (all → ~1.0 at bias_rel 1.0) → generic yes-bias,
  not concept-specific recovery (sole residue: forced concept@sub id ~0.18, almost all faint emotion-affect
  leakage, riding on FPR≈1.0). **Scope (reviewer-driven):** this is only the ADDITIVE detect-direction
  bias — a "say-yes" direction by construction, so uniform yes-bias is near-preordained; the literature's
  other two-stage-circuit form, **ablating (projecting out) the deny/gate direction**, was NOT tried (a
  Segment-10 mechanistic follow-up). Precise claim: "the additive yes-bias lever fails," not "the
  two-stage-circuit lever is exhausted."
  → No output-independent concept-specific detection is observed; priming, forced-choice (validated
  channels), and the additive detection-bias lever do not recover one. Elicitation is well-tried but not
  literally exhaustive (deny-direction ablation untried).
  - **Grader is not leaking output-presence** (reviewer concern): among *fully* concept-biased outputs
    (graded presence=3), **63% report NO detection at all** (det≥1=0; 66% are not CLEAR detection) —
    drift, not a reported injected thought — and 77%+ of clear-detection answers contain explicit injection
    language — the detection grader separates
    "output is about the concept" from "model reports an injected thought", so the working-strength
    detection is not a grader artifact of concept-mention.
  - **First-token latent partial-decoupling:** the logit-lens P(Yes) is read at the answer position from
    the *latent state before any answer token is emitted/appended*; concept@sub gives ≈ 0 there too — so
    the sub-threshold null is not merely "the written answer lacks concept words", the latent disposition
    to say "Yes" is also absent.
  - **Sub is internally non-trivial:** concept@sub is **~15σ off-manifold along its own direction** (mean
    z_sub 14.9, median 13.9, range 1.3–32.3) yet presence ≈ 0.04 and detection = 0 — a concept-direction
    perturbation internally non-trivial but output-subthreshold and undetected (sharper than the
    random@working 50σ case). Caveat: at sub the concept's FUNCTIONAL impact is also ≈0, so this still
    does not separate an internal-reportability-threshold from output-reading (needs the output-clamping
    experiment).

**Limitations / alternative interpretations (reviewer-driven; do not overturn the outcome).**
- Output-presence is a *post-treatment mediator*: at a fixed layer, internal injection magnitude and
  output-presence rise together, so conditioning on presence cannot fully separate "detection requires a
  minimum INTERNAL magnitude" from "detection requires OUTPUT bias". The honest claim is therefore: the
  data are **consistent with output-reading and provide NO evidence for output-independent introspection**
  (behavioral, latent, and forced-choice), but do **not strictly *prove*** an output-reading mechanism vs
  an internal-reportability-threshold account. (Partial mitigation: random@matched-projz has HIGH internal
  magnitude [50σ] yet LOW output-presence and is undetected, so high internal magnitude alone is not
  sufficient; the missing cell — a real concept injected internally-strong but output-suppressed — needs an
  **output-clamping / decoupling** experiment, deferred to Segment 4 because it needs a new GPU primitive
  that would invalidate the cached batteries.)
- **Stronger null worth stating:** the full pattern is also consistent with "32B has no privileged
  introspective channel — all apparent introspection is post-hoc rationalization of its own emerging
  output." The behavioral + latent + forced-choice nulls at sub-threshold support this as the parsimonious
  reading; PARTIAL is the label that credits the genuine working-strength content-specificity — the
  operative conclusion for Segment 4 is "no output-independent introspection on 32B" (the stronger null is
  equally consistent with the data).

**Elicitation-lever clause:** not triggered (forced-choice sub diff-in-diff CI spans 0; detection-bias
is pure yes-bias). Verdict unchanged.

### OUTCOME: **PARTIAL** — concept-specific but **output-dependent** (NOT "anomaly-only", NOT genuine
output-independent introspection).
Components 1 + 2 PASS at working strength; Component 3 FAILS. The working-strength detection is
**content/subspace-specific** (it refutes the generic raw-off-manifoldness anomaly-detection alternative
— content-free vectors at matched internal off-manifoldness give ~0) AND identification-specific, **but
it is gated by output-presence** (detection ∝ presence; ≈0 at matched-low presence). At a sub-threshold /
output-matched operating point — the regime the project cares about — concept detection = perturbation
detection = FPR ≈ 0, behaviorally, latently (first-token P(Yes)≈0), and under forced-choice. The honest
mechanistic reading: the result is **consistent with output-reading and shows no evidence of
output-independent introspection** (the autoregressive caveat made quantitative — the residual stream
partly IS the impending output), while not strictly excluding an internal-reportability-threshold account
(see Limitations). Either way, **no output-independent concept-specific introspection is demonstrated on
32B**, which is what governs Segment 4.

## Part C — Segment-4 recommendation + model-reconsideration call

**The replication does NOT abort the project.** The PARTIAL outcome governs HOW Segment 4 proceeds:

1. **The content-free perturbation baseline + naturalistic/on-manifold (activation-patched) injection
   condition are now MANDATORY in Segment 4** (not optional). The battery here validates the
   content-free baseline as a clean anomaly control (random@matched-projz detection ≈ 0), and shows a
   positive discrimination result must be checked against it. The naturalistic/on-manifold condition is
   needed because the off-manifold diff-of-means injection's effect is dominated by its output-bias.
1b. **Run the output-clamping / pre-output decoupling experiment (the decisive cell this segment could
   not run).** The one experiment that would convert "consistent with output-reading" into a clean verdict
   is: inject a concept internally-strong but PREVENT it from reaching the output (clamp/suppress the
   concept's logits during generation, or read the introspective disposition strictly from the
   pre-first-token latent), and test whether detection survives. It was deferred here only because it
   needs a new GPU primitive that would invalidate S3's cached batteries; it is cheap in a fresh Segment-4
   harness and is **the highest-value single experiment** for settling output-independence. (Partial S3
   evidence already in hand: the first-token latent P(Yes)≈0 at sub; and the cross-layer check shows a
   coherent, fully concept-biased L40 output is NOT flagged — so it is not *pure* output-text-reading, but
   detection still never appears without output-presence.) Treat output-clamping as MANDATORY alongside
   the controls in (1).
2. **Matched-salience on OUTPUT-PRESENCE is the load-bearing axis** (operationally: the validated S1
   concept-presence grader on the downstream generation). Detection here is a monotonic function of graded
   output-presence (0.00 → 0.34 as presence 0 → 3) and collapses to the floor at matched-low presence. So
   Segment 4's prompt-vs-activation comparison MUST be matched on downstream output concept-presence (the
   behavioral salience axis), with projection-z / post-RMSNorm Δ as secondary internal axes (this battery
   shows projection-z ≠ functional impact — a 50σ random vector is
   coherent and undetected — so internal-axis matching alone is insufficient; match output-presence).
3. **Use the discrimination benchmark as a more sensitive instrument, but do not expect output-
   independent introspection on 32B.** The free-text, forced-choice, AND latent channels all show zero
   sub-threshold concept-specific signal. Segment 4 should: (a) lead with the forced-choice 2AFC
   source-attribution (prompt vs injection) since it is the project's core design and a different, more
   constrained measure; (b) emphasize the SUB-threshold / output-matched operating point; (c) state
   plainly that an above-chance discrimination at output-biasing strength is consistent with
   output-reading and does NOT establish introspection unless it survives the matched-salience +
   content-free-perturbation + on-manifold controls and holds sub-threshold.
4. **What a Segment-4 result would license:** a positive discrimination that (i) survives output-presence
   matching, (ii) exceeds the content-free perturbation baseline, (iii) holds for naturalistic/on-manifold
   injection, and (iv) appears sub-threshold → genuine source-attribution introspection. Absent (i)–(iv),
   a positive result reduces to salience/anomaly/output-reading and must be framed as such.

**Model reconsideration: STAY on Qwen2.5-32B (do not escalate before Segment 4).** Reasoning:
- The bottleneck is NOT capability-elicitation under-trying — priming (S2), forced-choice, and a
  detection-bias vector were all tried; the null at sub-threshold is robust across behavioral, latent,
  and forced-choice channels. The limiting factor is the **autoregressive output-reading confound**,
  which is structural, not obviously model-size-specific.
- Escalation cost is high: Qwen2.5-72B (~145 GB bf16) needs 2×GPU or quantization AND a full
  re-derivation + re-validation of the S1 concept-vector library on the new model. The literature's
  strongest introspection result was **closed Claude** (not injectable), so escalating an *open* model is
  not guaranteed to clear the bar.
- The discrimination benchmark (Segment 4) is a different, potentially more sensitive instrument and is
  cheaper to try first on 32B. If Segment 4's matched-salience forced-choice is at chance on 32B even
  with the mandatory controls, THAT is the point to revisit escalation (with concrete evidence).
- **Concrete recommendation:** proceed to Segment 4 on 32B with the mandatory anomaly/on-manifold/
  matched-salience controls; revisit a 72B escalation only if the Segment-4 pilot is clearly
  capability-limited (not confound-limited).

**Considering geometry data**

I need to use s6_geometry, which seems to suggest aggregating data at the bottom. Maybe I should read it and use grep to filter the necessary information. content.thinking:
**Clarifying findings from the run**

The user is asking about missing important findings, particularly regarding a cross-model caveat about Llama's concept directions being less orthogonal, with a comprehension of 0.80. There seems to be an exception with Llama’s near-orthogonal robustness. The final writeup also mentions secondary cell CI spanned chance but didn't pass. Moreover, I’m wondering if details regarding the B5 patch at L40 may need more context since the results note symmetry and an anomaly at deeper layers. Is this a significant finding?

**Analyzing patch classification complexities**

It seems complicated that "naturalistic patches were called injection only 0.45" at S5, while at L40, an on-manifold patch indicates injection around 0.996, not being treated as a prompt. The final numbers audit mentions this at S6, emphasizing the concept presence being higher than noted. I’m wondering if this missing nuance could impact the conclusion, as patch source judgments vary with layers. It complicates how we view naturalistic patches and their relationship to prompts—could this alter our understanding of model sensitivity?

**Examining patch anomalies and findings**

At L40, the naturalistic patch appeared as an injection, possibly due to a high-salience layer that overwrites context. The final audit flags this detail, making me question if it should be included in the writeup since it feels significant. It looks like the discussion about layer depth and salience cues only touches on symmetric tests, not the important patch flip mentioned. Another potential oversight concerns internal versus output dissociation results in §6C, showing within-layer projections affecting source choices. I think these details warrant mention in the review!

**Evaluating findings for inclusion**

It looks like the internal z regression shows a weak positive after output presence, which might not be mentioned in the final writeup. I'm wondering if it supports the idea that internal salience can be read weakly but is dominated by the prompt. There are also notes about S5’s latent variation related to emotion and object positivity that could be significant. Additionally, comprehension metrics for Llama and Qwen72 aren't included—maybe they should be if they demonstrate instrument reliability. There’s a lot to consider about what is crucial for clarity!

**Identifying potential missing findings**

The user is concerned about missing important findings. I think there's no major gap, but I would flag the need for exact positive control numbers in Results or Appendix C, specifically the prompt-literal 1.00 and inject-sub 0.89, to substantiate instrument reliability. It seems the Appendix C table doesn't include these controls, only listing claims. While the methods mention positive controls, the absence of specific numbers could be significant. Additionally, raw summaries concerning output presence and concept numbers aren’t in the final writeup, which seems like another potential omission!

**Evaluating report details and verification**

I’m assessing if the final writeup is missing anything significant, like details on "reproducibility verification" related to cached data. While Appendix A mentions committed summaries, it doesn’t specify cached verification, which could be fine. I also need to check whether the final writeup refers to an "AI agent"—it doesn't, but it does mention "AI Introspection," which is acceptable. I should confirm that all plots are saved properly in both PNG and PDF formats since figure links only provide PNG files. Noting these details seems helpful!

**Assessing plot display issues**

I need to check if `make_final_plots.py` correctly uses `fig.tight_layout()` and `bbox_inches='tight'`, but it seems the images might still be clipped due to narrow margins. In the plots, the y-axis label is rotated, and the string "Fraction of first-token choices" appears cut off at the top. It seems the cropping might be due to the layout with the legend placed outside. Since labels appear truncated, this is a real problem. I should recommend adjusting the figure height or margins to fix the clipping!

**Examining plot and legend layout**

The legend below the plots might be consuming vertical space, leading to a compressed look. Longer legend labels are readable but could be optimized. In Fig 1, the y-axis label "choices" isn't fully visible, which is a concern. While the plots are mostly clean and understandable, the absence of uncertainty intervals in Figures 1 and 2 could weaken their academic robustness. Adding 95% CIs or showing exact counts would enhance clarity. I should also check the final writeup to ensure the figure claims align with the plots.

**Clarifying plot caption discrepancies**

The caption states "over same grid," but it seems to not refer to the same strengths. It mentions "Orange bars show best aligned positive control," yet if the blue bars represent tested layers and strengths, the orange only shows working alignment. For Qwen72, the sub-aligned max is 1.0, but the plot shows 0.97, which implies a calculation issue. Similarly, for Qwen32, the values might be inaccurately presented. The caption might need adjustments to reflect the true alignment and testing context accurately.

**Addressing plot captions and strengths**

Upon reviewing Figure 1 in make_final_plots, it comments that the "best aligned control over the same grid," but actually focuses on working only. The code selects working rates, while the text lists "best aligned-control rates" without mentioning strengths, which could be misleading. I need to highlight that the caption should clarify this or compute a comparable best across both strengths. For Figure 2, the caption for salience doesn't mention "sub-threshold," even though the results do. Clarifying this in both the plot and the legend would help avoid any confusion.

**Clarifying plot strengths and comparisons**

I noticed an important mismatch in strengths in Figure 2, which overlays source test working and salience sub—these are different strengths. The results text indicates a salience peak at L40, but the plot doesn’t label these strengths, which could mislead viewers. I need to flag this. For Figure 1, the source max is blue over all strengths, but the caption doesn’t clarify this. I should specify that it includes best primary opposed both sub and working. Lastly, Figure 3 mentions "output-MATCHED" but might need an explicit mention of the "external linear probe" context.

**Reviewing figure clarity and structure**

In the figure, the axis only shows layers 40, 48, and 56, while the text mentions the oracle performance at every layer, which isn't fully supported by the plot since it only reflects a subset. The caption states "consistently below oracle" for those layers, but to clarify, I could add that the figure is representative of layers 40-56. Additionally, I'll need to verify the cleanliness and labels of the plot. I'm also wondering about image dimensions—are they low? Lastly, the academic structure seems fine, with appendices correctly positioned after references.

**Assessing report clarity and structure**

I think I could mention that the "Methods" section is high-level, and while it serves the final report well, it omits specific sample sizes, layers, and strengths, which are in the appendix. Adding this information would enhance clarity. The user is concerned about maintaining an academic structure, and I'd agree that it largely meets the requirements, though some terms like "pre-registered criterion" and "source criterion" need definitions. To avoid over-claiming, I might suggest softening phrases, like changing "explained by" to "consistent with." Overall, I should ensure consistency with key numbers across reports.

**Spot-checking figures and numbers**

I've done some spot-checking of the final write-up numbers and found matches across multiple sections. For example, S2 shows values of 0.104/0.028 and S3 has 0.208 vs 0/0.010. However, I noticed potential inconsistencies. Specifically, in Figure 1, the orange bar representing the working max appears to misalign with the blue bar that represents all strengths. If interpreted literally, Qwen32's aligned max should be 0.906, yet the report seems to specify working numbers. Also, Figure 2 should label the overlay, and there's ambiguity around injection values needing clarification in the scope.

**Reviewing figures and metrics**

I’m reflecting on several aspects of the findings. For example, Figure 3's axis label "Held-out balanced accuracy" aligns with section s8b, which seems consistent. The phrase regarding "random or shuffled vectors matched on projection magnitude" appears okay based on the perturb_summary. Also, while Figure 1 presents Qwen72's aligned controls as 0.97, the final audit shows a max of 0.9688 at L56. There's also a statement in Results 1 about no-injection false positives being 0/120, which matches the final audit's FPR. I need to check the final write-up, especially "Appendix C," for the full audit.

**Checking artifact paths and instructions**

I notice a potential issue with the final writeup in /workspace. The released artifact path is mentioned as `/source...`, but I can't find the `results/final_numbers_audit.md` file there. Appendix A states that paths reference the artifact directory `phase_segment_9_phase_0`, and while Appendix C suggests `results/final_numbers_audit.md` might be okay as an artifact, it doesn’t seem to exist if final_writeup stands alone. Since user is asking to review ./final_writeup.md and ./final_plots, I might need to clarify path contexts in INSTRUCTIONS or the overall plan. It could be beneficial to review those documents for original academic structure as well.

content.text:
---
name: "s9_final_synthesis"
description: "Terminal synthesis: consolidate the whole project (S0-S8) into a single standalone, paper-like final report that answers the core question with carefully-bounded caveats; verify end-to-end reproducibility (--assert-cached) and audit that every headline number traces to a committed results file; produce a coherent publication-quality figure set from existing cached data; run the empirical-ml-research Task-Completion-Checklist reviews (research-reviewer / data-inspector / query_all_models) and fold in fixes. NO new experiments / no new GPU runs / no new models."
---

# Instructions

## 0. What this overall project is (read carefully — this motivates everything)

This project investigates **whether a language model can genuinely introspect on its own internal
states** — specifically, **can a model distinguish a concept being *injected into its activations* from
the same concept merely being *present in its prompt*?** (Prompt route: add "cat" tokens. Activation
route: add/graft a "cat" steering vector into the residual stream at some layer L.) A reliable, fair,
above-chance discrimination of the *source* of an internal state — **not** explained by salience
mismatch, generic anomaly/off-manifold detection, the model reading its own already-biased output, or
prompt-inference (re-reading / semantic inference about the input) — would be evidence for genuine
introspective access to the source/nature of an internal state. `OVERALL_PLAN.md` (in your working
directory) and `proposal.md` have the full motivation.

**All the science is done (Segments 0–8 are COMPLETE).** This is **Segment 9, the terminal synthesis
segment.** Your job is NOT to run new experiments — it is to **consolidate the entire project into a
single, trustworthy, carefully-bounded final report**, verify it reproduces, finalize the figures, and
have it reviewed. The project's value rests on its **rigor and honesty**; your deliverable must preserve
both (neither over-claim the negative nor bury the carefully-stated bounds).

## 1. Where we are — the full arc (Segments 0–8 are done; READ THESE FIRST)

Read the top-level `writeups/write_up.md` (the 9 phase takeaways) and, as needed, the phase write-ups
below. **You must read enough to write an accurate, coherent synthesis — default to thoroughness.**
- `writeups/write_up.md` — **the consolidated phase-by-phase log (S0→S8 Phase 1). READ FIRST.**
- `writeups/write_up_s5_discrimination_full.md` — the headline behavioral answer + the de-confounded
  5a/5b/5c battery + the matched-salience methodology + the bounds.
- `writeups/write_up_s6_layer_sweep.md` — the layer-invariant negative + the representational-geometry
  premise test + the bounded-positive (internal-vs-output) dissociation + the CoT result.
- `writeups/write_up_s7_cross_model.md` — the cross-model generalization (Llama-3.3-70B + Qwen2.5-72B)
  + the injection-effectiveness controls + the structural-limiter conclusion.
- `writeups/write_up_s8_mechanistic_probe.md` (Phase 0) + `writeups/write_up_s8_phase1_symmetric_probe.md`
  (Phase 1) — the representational source probe (no decodable source beyond graft/anomaly; the
  non-identifiability of source vs relative-internal-magnitude) + the deny-direction ablation failure.
- `writeups/write_up_s4_benchmark_design_pilot.md`, `write_up_s3_confounds_validity.md`,
  `write_up_s2_introspection_replication.md`, `write_up_s1_concept_library.md`,
  `write_up_s0_infra_derisk.md` — design/gate/replication/library/infra.
- `writeups/benchmark_design_s4.md` — the benchmark design doc + outcome-licensing (i)–(v).
- `writeups/continuation_context.md` — how to run everything; the pipeline; caching gotchas; the
  indexing convention; tribal knowledge. The `prereg_s*.md` files are the per-segment pre-registrations.

**The project's headline findings (what the synthesis must convey accurately):**
- **The core answer is NO.** Across the full 46-concept set, all injection layers (L0→L56), three open
  models (`Qwen2.5-32B`, `Qwen2.5-72B`, `Llama-3.3-70B`), and verbal-2AFC / first-token-latent /
  chain-of-thought readouts, the model shows **no source discrimination above the salience / anomaly /
  output-reading / prompt-inference floors.** The decisive **5a opposed-emphasis** test (emphasize one
  concept in the prompt, inject the other at matched magnitude) picks the **injected** concept **< 0.5 at
  every layer×model** (global max ~0.42–0.495); none survive BH-FDR + the CI-lower>0.55 bar.
- **The injection is demonstrably effective** (it is NOT a failed injection): aligned emphasize+inject
  ≈0.70–0.74; matched-norm random presence ~0.1 vs concept ~1.5–1.9. The injection pulls the readout
  toward itself but **cannot be attributed to source**.
- **What the model CAN read** (the *bounded positive*) = **relative salience / internal-magnitude /
  anomaly-status that grows with depth** — NOT source. The proposal's "early≈prompt, late-diverge"
  geometry holds, but reinterpreted: divergence = salience/anomaly, not source-attribution; and a direct
  representational-geometry measurement shows the premise itself doesn't cleanly hold (the injection is an
  off-manifold, louder state, barely concept-aligned with the prompt state).
- **The mechanism / representational level (S8):** an external **linear** probe cannot decode
  prompt-vs-injection *source* from the residual beyond re-reading-the-prompt + concept-agnostic
  graft/anomaly (Phase 0); and in the balanced two-concept design (Phase 1) "which was injected" is
  **non-identifiable** from "which is more internally active" (injection necessarily raises internal
  magnitude). The deny-direction ablation (the two-stage-circuit lever) does not recover concept-specific
  detection.
- **Interpretation:** the limiter is **structural** (the autoregressive "the residual stream partly *is*
  the impending output" output-reading confound + the off-manifold injection method), not a 32B-specific
  capability deficit (a more-capable model and a scale-up both fail).
- **The contribution is two-fold:** (1) a rigorous, fair, **pre-registered, cross-model** negative answer
  to a sharp introspection question; (2) a reusable **methodology** for separating genuine introspective
  source-attribution from the confounds (matched-salience load-bearing on output-presence; content-free +
  on-manifold-patch anomaly controls; held-out concepts; prompt-inference / paraphrase controls;
  instrument validation via positive controls; first-token-latent output-severing; pre-registration +
  outcome-licensing + MC-aware criteria).

## 2. Goal of THIS segment (Segment 9) — synthesis, reproducibility, review

Produce a **trustworthy, standalone, carefully-bounded final report** and make the whole project a clean,
reproducible artifact. Concretely:
1. **A comprehensive standalone final report** (§3) — the centerpiece.
2. **End-to-end reproducibility verification + a headline-numbers audit** (§4).
3. **A coherent publication-quality figure set** assembled from existing cached results (§5).
4. **The full Task-Completion-Checklist review pass** (research-reviewer / data-inspector /
   `query_all_models`) on the final synthesis, with fixes folded in (§6).

**This is consolidation, NOT new science.** Do **NOT**: run new GPU experiments / new model runs / new
probes / new injection methods; build the on-manifold sub-span/interpolated graft (a documented future
direction, NOT this segment); re-run the committed S0–S8 results; **edit `steering_modal.py`** (it
invalidates ALL Modal caches via `code_hash` — the committed results must stay reproducible). Cross-
segment *consolidation* analyses that reuse already-cached results/JSON (e.g., a unified floors table, a
master figure assembled from existing per-segment numbers) ARE in scope; new model/GPU computation is
not.

**Skills:** `empirical-ml-research` and `caching` are on by default. You will mostly be writing,
plotting from cached data, auditing numbers, and running review subagents.

## 3. The comprehensive final report (the centerpiece)

Write a single, **standalone, paper-like** final report at **`writeups/final_report.md`** (and update the
top-level `writeups/write_up.md` to open with a short executive summary + a pointer to it). It must be
readable by someone who has **not** seen the phase logs — a coherent narrative, not a concatenation of
phase write-ups. Required content (organize sensibly; these are the must-cover elements):

- **The question + motivation** (the prompt-route vs activation-route distinction; why source-attribution
  would be evidence of genuine introspection; the four confounds the design must defeat: salience
  mismatch, anomaly/off-manifold detection, output-reading, prompt-inference).
- **The headline answer**, stated up front and **carefully bounded**: *no source-attribution introspection
  above the salience/anomaly/output-reading/prompt-inference floors, under the injection methods (diff-of-
  means + position-for-position sequence patch), layers, readouts (verbal-2AFC / first-token-latent / CoT),
  and models (Qwen2.5-32B/72B, Llama-3.3-70B) tested* — with the bounded relative-salience positive, and
  NOT a metaphysical "the model fundamentally cannot introspect on source."
- **The methodology / validity scaffolding** (foreground this — it is half the contribution): the steering
  pipeline + concept-vector library; the introspection-detection protocol; the discrimination benchmark
  (controlled 2AFC, explicit-source, 5a opposed-emphasis, the first-token latent readout); **multi-axis
  matched salience, load-bearing on output-presence** (projection-z ≠ functional impact); the **mandatory
  content-free perturbation baseline + on-manifold activation-patched condition**; the trivial-prompt-
  reading / paraphrase control; **instrument validation via positive controls + comprehension**;
  pre-registration + outcome-licensing (i)–(v) + the MC-aware criterion; concept-clustered CIs.
- **Falsifiability & power (make this explicit — so the negative reads as a genuine, powered null, not an
  unfalsifiable one).** State, up front, **what a *positive* result would have looked like** (a probe /
  2AFC beating the salience/anomaly/output-reading/prompt-inference floors AND satisfying the
  pre-registered outcome-licensing (i)–(v) + the CI-lower>0.55 / MC-aware bars), and that the instruments
  **had the power to detect it**: the injection is demonstrably effective (aligned emphasize+inject
  ~0.70–0.74; matched-norm random presence ~0.1 vs concept ~1.5–1.9), the positive controls / readout
  pass (e.g. the S8 "injected vs none" probe 0.93–1.00; comprehension ~1.00), and the aligned/salience arms
  ARE above chance — so the source-specific null is a real absence of source signal, not a dead instrument.
- **The results arc** (a coherent through-line, with the key numbers + CIs): S0 infra de-risk → S1 the
  32B library (50/51 concepts steer) → S2 replication (weak + output-confounded; priming ~3.7×) → S3 the
  pre-registered gate = PARTIAL (concept-specific + identification-specific, but **output-dependent**; no
  output-independent introspection) → S4 the benchmark design + the pre-registered **kill criterion
  triggered** (K2 anomaly + K3 salience/prompt-inference: no instrument isolates source) → S5 full-scale
  **negative** (5a opposed picks-injected ~0.39; 5b synthetic→"injection" but the on-manifold patch read
  "prompt"; 5c the internal inconsistency) → S6 **layer-invariant** + the geometry premise doesn't hold →
  S7 **cross-model general** (two families + scale-up + capability) → S8 the **representational** verdict
  (no linearly-decodable source beyond graft/anomaly; source ≡ relative-internal-magnitude is
  non-identifiable; deny-ablation fails).
- **The bounded positive, stated precisely:** the model/residual carries a weak, **prompt-text-dominated**
  relative-internal-magnitude / anomaly cue (which co-present concept is internally more active, for
  affective/object concepts), which is **NOT** introspective source-attribution and is dominated by prompt
  salience under opposed emphasis.
- **What each result licenses + the comprehensive, honest BOUNDS** (a dedicated section). Carry ALL the
  standing bounds: model/method/readout-specific (32B/72B/70B; diff-of-means + sequence-patch injection;
  verbal-2AFC/latent/CoT readouts); **70–72B open models are below frontier / closed Claude** (the
  literature's strongest introspection was closed + non-injectable) — a frontier-capability positive is
  not ruled out; **linear / single-MLP probe ≠ all probes** (relational/attention-circuit source could
  exist outside a single-token residual readout); **off-manifold / graft non-identifiability** (a coherent
  on-manifold injected state is *prompt-like by construction*; the on-manifold balanced design is **not
  constructible with the full-context sequence-patch primitive tested** — sub-span/interpolated grafts are
  untried, a noted future direction); the **non-identifiability by-construction** (injection necessarily
  raises internal magnitude, so "source" and "relative internal magnitude" cannot be dissociated in the
  injection paradigm); the autoregressive output-reading limit.
- **Future work** (what would change/extend the conclusion): frontier + closed introspection-tuned models;
  on-manifold sub-span/interpolated grafts (a both-concepts-preserving on-manifold injection); non-linear /
  relational / attention-circuit probes; trained-/fine-tuned-readout probes; alternative injection methods;
  introspection-specific fine-tuning.
- **Relation to prior work (an explicit, structured section — required).** Concretely situate the
  project's findings against the key prior work the project was framed around (OVERALL_PLAN §2), stating
  for each how our result relates (replicates / extends / qualifies / operationalizes):
  - **Anthropic "Signs of introspection in LLMs"** (~20% detection on closed Claude): we replicate *weak*
    detection on open models but show it is **output-dependent** (collapses to the FPR floor
    sub-threshold) and does **not** isolate source-attribution.
  - **"Latent Introspection" (Qwen-32B)**: the **priming boost replicates** (~3.7× on 32B; ~3.0× on 72B)
    but does **not** yield genuine sub-threshold detection, and the logit-lens "latent detection" is
    **output-presence-gated** (≈0 sub-threshold).
  - **"Mechanisms of Introspective Awareness" (the two-stage evidence-carrier → deny-gate circuit)**: the
    **deny-direction ABLATION fails** (projection-out inert; additive = a wholesale yes-bias), so on these
    models the data do **not** support a recoverable suppressed-detection signal behind a deny gate.
  - **"Dissociating Direct Access from Inference"**: our prompt-vs-activation benchmark **operationalizes**
    this distinction and finds the apparent discrimination is **inference** (salience / anomaly /
    prompt-reading), with **no demonstrated direct access** to the source of an internal state.
  Use this section to bound the novelty claim honestly (what is genuinely new = the fair, pre-registered,
  cross-model, de-confounded *negative* + the methodology; what merely replicates prior observations).
- **An honest statement of the contribution and what it means for the introspection literature.**

**CRITICAL GUARDS (the synthesis must honor the project's own corrections — do not regress):**
- Do **NOT** over-harden the negative into "source is not represented anywhere." It is a **bounded /
  non-identifiability** negative (see the bounds above).
- Do **NOT** cite, as evidence, the artifacts two reviewers corrected in S8 Phase 1: the **opposed-arm
  0.50** (degenerate by construction — identical residuals under both labels) and the **salience-probe
  gap** (definitional where z-separation is large). The S8 representational negative rests on: the
  source-probe staying **below the concept-aware proj-z magnitude oracle** at every non-degenerate cell +
  on the pre-registered salience-matched subset; the **non-identifiability argument**; the **positive
  control** (the readout has power, 0.93–1.00); the **patch erasure**; and **Phase-0**.
- Characterize the **bounded positive accurately** (relative magnitude / anomaly, prompt-dominated — NOT
  introspection of source).
- Every headline number must match a committed `results/*.json` (§4 audit).

## 4. Reproducibility verification + headline-numbers audit

- **`--assert-cached` end-to-end.** Verify the production pipeline reruns from cache (the project norm).
  Run the documented `--assert-cached` checks (see `continuation_context.md`); document the result. If any
  cell is not cache-clean, document precisely why (e.g., a known cold-start race) and whether it affects a
  headline number. **Do NOT edit `steering_modal.py`** (cache invalidation) and do **NOT** re-run the
  committed S0–S8 GPU results.
- **Headline-numbers audit.** For every headline number in the final report, confirm it traces to a
  committed results file (e.g., `results/s5_summary.json`, `s6_summary.json`,
  `s7_summary_{llama70b,qwen72b}.json`, `s8_probe_summary_matched_text.json`, `s8b_probe_summary.json`,
  the gate `results/replication_gate.md`, etc.). Produce a short **audit note** (e.g.,
  `results/final_numbers_audit.md`) mapping each headline claim → its source file + value. Fix any
  mismatch in the report. (This is the project's integrity norm; an S6/S8 reviewer caught real numeric
  misstatements before — be strict.)
- **Cost.** Consolidate total project cost from `total_cost.jsonl` and report it in the final report.

## 5. Final figures (from existing cached data only)

Produce a **coherent, publication-quality figure set** that tells the arc — assembled from the existing
per-segment results/plots (regenerate cleanly; do **not** run new experiments). A sensible set (adapt):
- A **headline cross-model figure**: the 5a opposed-emphasis picks-injected (< 0.5) across layers for
  32B/72B/70B vs the aligned + emphasis floors (the decisive source-vs-salience result).
- The **salience/anomaly-vs-layer** curves (relative-salience 2AFC peak + the on-manifold-patch
  "prompt"→"injection" anomaly onset) — the reinterpreted geometry.
- A **de-confounding / floors** figure: detection or discrimination vs the content-free / no-injection /
  on-manifold / matched-salience floors (the methodology made visual).
- The **representational probe** figure (S8): the source probe vs the positive control vs the proj-z
  magnitude oracle (the non-identifiability).
Ensure every figure is captioned, axis-labelled, and referenced from the final report. Keep prior
per-segment plots; these are the consolidated story figures.

## 6. Review pass (Task-Completion-Checklist) + wrap-up

- **Run the empirical-ml-research final reviews on the SYNTHESIS** (tell each reviewer the phase name
  `s9_final_synthesis` and that phase-specific write-ups are in use): a **`research-reviewer` /
  `research-plan-reviewer`** pass focused on **over-claim / bounds / numbers integrity** (does the report
  over-harden the negative? are all bounds carried? do numbers match committed results? is the bounded
  positive correctly characterized? are the S8-corrected artifacts kept out of the evidence?); a
  **data-inspection / `empirical-ml-data-inspector`-style** sanity pass that the headline claims are
  faithful to the inspected data; and **`query_all_models`** on the final headline framing + the bounds
  (is the negative stated at the right strength — neither over- nor under-claimed?). **Fold the fixes in**
  (don't just log them).
- **Documentation/wrap-up:** `writeups/final_report.md` (the standalone report); an updated top-level
  `writeups/write_up.md` (executive summary + pointer); `writeups/progress_log_s9_final_synthesis.md`
  (append-only chronology with timestamps + git hashes; `TZ='America/Los_Angeles' date '+%m/%d/%Y %H:%M'`);
  keep `writeups/continuation_context.md` current (final state; how to reproduce; the figure/audit
  locations). Track any (small) API cost from reviews in `total_cost.jsonl`. **Git:** commit your work.

## 7. Deliverables (definition of done)

1. **`writeups/final_report.md`** — a standalone, paper-like synthesis: the question + motivation; the
   carefully-bounded headline answer; the methodology / validity scaffolding; the full results arc (with
   key numbers + CIs); the bounded positive; the reinterpreted geometric hypothesis; what each result
   licenses + the comprehensive bounds; future work; the contribution. Honors the §3 critical guards.
2. **Reproducibility verification + the headline-numbers audit** (`results/final_numbers_audit.md` or
   equivalent): `--assert-cached` result documented; every headline number traced to a committed results
   file; total cost consolidated.
3. **A coherent publication-quality figure set** assembled from existing cached data, captioned and
   referenced from the report.
4. **The Task-Completion-Checklist review pass** done (research-reviewer + data-inspection +
   `query_all_models`) with fixes folded in.
5. Updated `writeups/write_up.md` (executive summary + pointer), `progress_log_s9_final_synthesis.md`,
   `continuation_context.md`, cost tracking, committed git state.

**Honest-framing note (important).** The project's central value is a **rigorously-established, fair,
cross-model NEGATIVE** plus the **methodology** that makes it trustworthy. The synthesis must state the
negative at exactly the right strength: **strong enough** (it is a clean, fair, pre-registered,
cross-model, layer-invariant, behaviorally + representationally converging result, with the injection
demonstrably effective and the confounds explicitly controlled) **and bounded enough** (model/method/
readout-specific; 70–72B below frontier; linear/relational/off-manifold/non-identifiability bounds;
a non-identifiability rather than a metaphysical absence). Do **not** inflate it into "models can't
introspect," and do **not** undersell it into "inconclusive." Report what the project actually
established, with its caveats — that honest calibration IS the deliverable. This is the terminal segment:
produce the final report, make it reproducible and reviewed, and stop.

# Overall Plan: Introspection Science — Injection vs. Prompt Discrimination

## 1. Core objective

This project investigates **whether language models can genuinely introspect on their own
internal states** — specifically, whether a model can **distinguish a concept being injected
into its activations from the same concept merely being present in its prompt.**

The intuition (from `proposal.md`): consider two ways of putting "cat" into a model's
processing:
- **Prompt route:** add "cat" tokens to the input text.
- **Activation route:** add a "cat" steering vector to the residual stream at some layer.

If you inject the steering vector at the *first* layer, the two are nearly identical. The
interesting scientific question is **how they diverge when the steering vector is injected at
later layers, and whether the model can tell the difference.** If a model can reliably
discriminate "this concept is in my activations because something was injected" from "this
concept is in my input because I read it," that is evidence for introspective
access to the *source/nature* of an internal state — not merely re-reading the prompt. If it
cannot discriminate, then much of the apparent "introspection" in the literature may reduce to
steering-altered prompt interpretation.

**Important interpretive caveat (decide framing carefully, see §3–§4).** A difference-of-means
vector added uniformly to every position is *off-distribution* (static, position-agnostic,
inconsistent with neighboring activations), whereas a prompt-borne concept is contextualized and
on-manifold. So "discrimination above chance" is **also** consistent with the model merely
detecting that *its activations are weird / off-manifold* (generic perturbation/anomaly
detection) rather than performing genuine source-attribution of a concept. A naive positive
result would over-claim. The benchmark must therefore be designed so that a positive result
isn't explained by anomaly detection alone (e.g., via naturalistic/on-manifold injections and a
content-free perturbation baseline — see Segment 4), and the write-up must state precisely what
each outcome licenses. Relatedly, because the activation-route vector is *derived from*
prompt-presence and the two routes coincide at layer 0 *by construction*, "divergence at later
layers" is partly a statement about how token-derived representations get contextualized through
the stack — frame it as such rather than as automatic proof of introspection.

The project has two top-level parts, in order:

1. **Replicate** the concept-injection introspection result on an **open-weight model** and
   build confidence that the effect is real (not an artifact like yes-bias, anomaly detection,
   or confabulation).
2. **Build and run the discrimination benchmark**: can the model discriminate concept-in-prompt
   from concept-in-activations? Then **run many ablations** (prompt-injection variants, injection
   layer, injection strength / salience matching, model scale/family) to understand how robust
   the result is and what drives it.

## 2. Background / what the literature says (carry this context into every phase)

- **Anthropic, "Signs of introspection in LLMs":** find an activation pattern for a concept
  (e.g., "all caps"), inject it into the residual stream in an unrelated context, ask the model
  whether it notices an injected thought. The compelling successes are when the model reports
  something internal is off and *identifies* the concept *before* the concept has obviously
  biased its output. Best protocols work only ~20% of the time and are very sensitive to
  injection strength. Claude Opus 4/4.1 did best.
- **"Latent Introspection" (Qwen 32B):** models often *deny* injection in sampled text, but
  logit-lens reveals latent detection signals. **Prompting the model with accurate information
  about the introspection mechanism raises injection sensitivity from ~0.3% to ~39.9%** with only
  a small false-positive increase. (This prompting trick is essential for getting a measurable
  effect on open models.)
- **"Mechanisms of Introspective Awareness":** argues for a two-stage circuit — early
  "evidence-carrier" features detect perturbations; downstream "gate" features implement a default
  *negative* answer. Detection and concept-identification are partly distinct. Refusal ablation or
  a trained bias vector improves detection with small false-positive cost.
- **"Dissociating Direct Access from Inference":** closest prior work — separates prompt-anomaly
  *inference* from *direct access* to internal states. Our project makes the distinction sharper
  by directly comparing concept tokens in the prompt against concept vectors injected into
  activations.

Recurring takeaways to keep in mind:
- There is real evidence some models compute functions of internal perturbations.
- **Detection is more robust than semantic identification.**
- **Early-layer interventions are easier to detect than later-layer ones.**
- Post-training and prompting strongly affect whether introspective info reaches the output.
- Some apparent introspection is explained by artifacts (affirmative-response bias, anomaly
  detection, confabulated concept guesses) — controlling for these is central.

## 3. Strategy and key design decisions

**De-risk fastest first.** The single biggest unknown is the *infrastructure*: can we extract
concept vectors and inject them into an open model's residual stream on rentable GPUs, and does
steering actually work? Segment 0 settles this on a small/cheap model before any science.

**Then de-risk the science.** Replication (Segments 2–3) must produce a *trustworthy* effect
with proper baselines before we invest in the novel discrimination benchmark. If we cannot get a
real introspection signal on an open model, the discrimination question is premature — and the
phase planner should adapt (try stronger models, better prompting, the refusal-ablation/bias-vector
tricks, or escalate).

**The discrimination benchmark is the core contribution; its design is the crux.** The hardest
methodological problem is making a **fair, matched comparison** between "concept in prompt" and
"concept in activations." The result can be trivially confounded by salience mismatch (e.g., a
strong injection vs. a faint prompt mention, or vice versa). Segment 4 is a dedicated design+pilot
segment with heavy "are we measuring what we intend to measure?" scrutiny. Two design contracts
the Segment-4 planner must honor:
- **Salience matching is multi-axis.** Concept "strength" can be matched on several axes:
  behavioral (graded **downstream output concept-presence**), logit-level (**target-token logprob
  lift** / next-token KL), and internal (a **linear-probe readout** of "concept present" at a
  downstream layer). Matching one axis generally leaves others mismatched, and the model could
  discriminate on whichever axis is mismatched. So match on multiple axes and **report how the
  headline result moves with the matching metric** (don't pick one arbitrary defensible-looking
  metric and stop).
- **Anomaly-detection controls (see the §1 caveat).** Include a **naturalistic / on-manifold
  injection** condition (e.g., activation patching from a real forward pass that actually contained
  the concept, not only a synthetic mean vector) and a **content-free perturbation baseline** (can
  the model discriminate a random/irrelevant vector from no injection, just as well?). If the model
  discriminates *any* injection equally well, the benchmark is measuring perturbation detection,
  not concept source-attribution.

**The interesting regime is sub-threshold.** The compelling introspection result (and the
proposal's framing) is the model detecting/identifying the concept *before its output is visibly
biased*. At output-biasing strengths, "detection" can be trivial (the model just reads its own
already-biased output). So replication and discrimination metrics should emphasize the
**sub-threshold / pre-output-bias** operating point, not only the strength that visibly steers
output.

**Two complementary discrimination framings** (decide/refine in Segment 4):
- **Detection-asymmetry (primary candidate):** reuse the introspection "do you detect an injected
  thought?" protocol and ask whether "injected-thought" reports track *actual injection* vs *mere
  prompt presence* of the concept. Genuine introspection ⇒ high "injected thought" rate under
  activation-injection but low under prompt-presence (the model should know the prompt concept is
  just input).
- **Explicit 2AFC discrimination:** on each trial the concept arrives via prompt OR via injection;
  ask the model to classify the source. Measure accuracy above chance.

**Infrastructure (verified for this environment):**
- No local GPU. **Use Modal for GPU work** (H100/A100 80GB). Keep the open model loaded once
  (e.g., a `modal.cls` with `@modal.enter`) and expose methods for batched activation extraction
  and steered generation; do not reload per call.
- **Activation steering: HF `transformers` + forward hooks** is the robust, size-agnostic default
  (register a residual-stream hook on decoder layers). `nnsight` is a clean alternative;
  `TransformerLens` is fine for small-model prototyping. Avoid vLLM for steering (hard to inject
  arbitrary activations); vLLM/Tinker are not needed here.
- **Concept vectors: difference-of-means** (mean residual at layer L over concept-bearing prompts
  minus matched neutral prompts), the standard, robust method.
- **Local machine** runs orchestration, LLM-judge grading (Opus 4.7 via Anthropic API),
  caching, and analysis. **Cache open-model generations** (keyed by prompt + full steering config
  + seed) as well as grader calls, so reruns are cheap and `--assert-cached` works.
- Models: de-risk infra on a small model (e.g., Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, single GPU); scale to a more
  capable model for the real replication (e.g., **Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct**, which the Latent
  Introspection work used). Treat exact model choices as revisable. Note: Qwen2.5-32B in bf16
  (~65 GB) fits one 80 GB H100/A100, but KV-cache + batching can get tight; Segments 2/9 may need
  2×GPU or quantization. **Build/validate the concept-vector library on the *main* model** (not
  the small de-risk model), since good vectors/layers/strengths are model-specific and validating
  on the small model then switching would force silent re-extraction (see Segment 1).
- Caveat on "keep warm": `modal.cls`/`@modal.enter` only keeps the model loaded *within* a single
  `app.run()`/container lifetime; across separate script invocations containers cold-start and
  reload. Generation/vector **caching** is what makes reruns cheap across invocations.
- Helper files (`cost_tracker.py`, `file_cache.py`, `llm_helper.py`, `get_research_gitignore.py`)
  are at **`/opt/abhay-pi/setup/ai_config/`** (the skills say `/home/ryan/documents/ai_config/`,
  which does not exist here). API keys are present as environment variables.

## 4. Risks and likely failure modes

- **Replication fails on open models.** The effect may be weak/absent without the right model
  scale, prompting, and strength calibration. Mitigation: start from the literature's recipe
  (introspection-priming prompt; sweep strength/layer; use a capable model; consider
  refusal-ablation / bias-vector boosts). Decide on the boundary between "we couldn't make it
  work" vs "the effect is genuinely small."
- **Injection-strength sensitivity.** Too weak → no effect; too strong → output is garbage /
  trivially dominated by the concept (no genuine introspection, just broken model). The usable
  window is narrow. Mitigation: careful log-scale strength sweeps with output-coherence checks.
- **Unfair prompt-vs-activation comparison (the central validity risk).** Conclusions can flip
  with salience matching, prompt phrasing/position, and injection layer/positions. Mitigation:
  Segment 4's explicit matched-salience design + Segment 8 robustness ablations.
- **Confounds masquerading as introspection:** affirmative/yes-bias, generic anomaly detection,
  confabulated concept guesses. Mitigation: no-injection and random-vector baselines, false-positive
  rates, pre-output timing analysis, logit-lens.
- **Off-manifold artifact = the discrimination benchmark's deepest validity threat.** A uniform
  diff-in-means injection is detectably "weird"; the model may discriminate it from prompt content
  by *anomaly detection* rather than *source-attribution of the concept*, which would invert the
  intended interpretation (see §1 caveat). Mitigation: naturalistic/on-manifold (activation-patched)
  injection condition + content-free perturbation baseline (Segment 4); be explicit about what each
  outcome licenses.
- **Trivial / super-threshold "detection."** Probing at output-biasing strengths can yield fake
  introspection (model reads its own biased output). Mitigation: emphasize sub-threshold operating
  points; pre-output timing analysis (Segment 3).
- **Weak statistics / grader-driven artifacts.** Headline claims can rest on too-few samples, an
  unvalidated grader, or many uncontrolled comparisons. Mitigation: adequate n per cell with CIs
  and an explicit chance baseline; validate graders against human labels before headline numbers;
  be aware of multiple comparisons across the many ablations.
- **"Not measuring what we want."** The gap between "can the model introspect?" and "what the eval
  actually scores" is easy to get wrong (grader leniency, format compliance, the model just naming
  whatever concept is salient). Mitigation: data inspection every experiment; `research-reviewer`
  measurement reviews.
- **Compute cost/time.** GPU hosting and large sweeps can be slow/expensive. Mitigation: cache
  generations, keep the model warm, batch, start small, scale deliberately.
- **Grader reliability.** Judging "did the model detect/identify/discriminate" is subtle.
  Mitigation: validate the grader against hand-labeled examples; inspect outputs.

prompt-dominance / output-reading.** The discrimination benchmark therefore proceeds as a
*characterization* instrument (lock the negative + the bounded relative-salience positive at scale),
not as a presumed-working source-attribution measure. The old standalone prompt-entry-variant

was elevated. The remaining segments are provisional and adjustable by future phase planners.

   for-position aligned donors, not positional cycling); counterbalanced diff-in-diff; per-concept
   held-out; scrutinize the bounded positive for output-reading.

- **Segment 5 — Full-scale discrimination characterization + the headline answer. STATUS: COMPLETE**
  (phase `s5_discrimination_full`). Ran the benchmark at full scale on 32B (46 robust concepts, 23
  cross-category pairs, L24) as a *characterization* instrument; implemented all S4-flagged refinements
  (matched salience EXERCISED by knob to common presence+coherence points; **position-for-position
  on-manifold patch** — now reaches a *coherent output-present on-manifold state*, presence 1.95, but
  the model treats it as prompt/conversation; counterbalanced diff-in-diff; per-concept clustered CIs;
  comprehension + coherence gates). Pre-registered (`prereg_s5.md`, committed before results) and
  review-hardened (query_all_models Claude/Gemini/GPT + research-plan-reviewer + a **decisive self-
  correction** via per-trial presence verification). **HEADLINE: NO reliable source discrimination
  above the salience/anomaly/output-reading/prompt-inference floors on 32B** (carefully bounded — a
  weak internal cue exists but is dominated by prompt-text; the cleanest on-manifold comparison is
  partly non-identifiable). Every apparent discrimination is a confound: 5a opposed-emphasis picks the
  injected concept only 0.39 < the prompt-emphasis floor (and a per-trial check showed the injected
  concept *dominates* output (presence 2.78) + internal state (~46σ) yet the model picks the prompt-
  emphasized one → readout follows prompt-text, not output/internal/source); 5b synthetic→"injection"
  0.95 vs on-manifold patch→"prompt" 0.45 despite 5× higher patch presence (anomaly/semantic-coherence
  gating); 5c the 2AFC reads that same patch as injected 0.73 (internal inconsistency = salience not
  source); the explicit channel is prompt-inference (paraphrase 0.996). Instrument valid (K5 PASS,
  comprehension 1.00). Bounded relative-salience positive = a weak prompt-dominated cue (emotion 0.74 /
  object 0.67; concrete/abstract ≈ chance; style inverted 0.19). Cost ≈ $60. **Two items explicitly
  deferred to Segment 6** (the reviewers' top follow-ups): (i) the **internal-projection-matched 2AFC**
  to cleanly separate the bounded positive's residual (+0.17 working pull) into internal-magnitude
  introspection vs output-prediction; (ii) **layer generalization** — the null is from one layer (L24).
  *Deliverable met:* the honest, CI-backed headline answer + bounds. Stay on 32B.

- **Segment 6 — Injection-layer sweep (the proposal's central geometric hypothesis) + the deferred
  bounded-positive dissociation.** Sweep the injection layer (early→mid→late, from the 15-layer library
  grid — per-layer vectors already extracted; no re-derivation) for the de-confounded battery (controlled
  2AFC; 5a opposed source test; 5b synthetic-vs-on-manifold-patch, patch captured+injected at L) to test

  input); later layers diverge — and whether the **S5 source-attribution negative is layer-invariant**.
  With the S4/S5 finding that the divergence the model *can* read is relative salience / anomaly,
  interpret the curves through that lens (does the synthetic→"injection" anomaly signature *emerge with

  discrimination/salience/anomaly-vs-layer curves + the layer-invariance verdict + the bounded-positive
  (internal vs output) verdict + the CoT verdict. Stay on 32B.
   **STATUS: COMPLETE** (phase `s6_layer_sweep`). **The S5 source-attribution negative is
   LAYER-INVARIANT** across the 10-layer grid L0→L56: the **5a opposed-emphasis** test picks the

  (the CENTERPIECE): can a linear classifier decode route (prompt vs injection) from the residual stream

  floor**; **held-out concepts**; a readout-layer sweep)? Either outcome is sharp: *decodable on the

  non-identifiability). *Deliverable:* the source-decodability verdict + the deny-ablation verdict +

   **STATUS: COMPLETE (Phase 0 + Phase 1).**

   + strict disjoint-distractor floors; held-out concepts) the probe decodes concept content + off-

   neutral AND distractor grafts = 1.00; Δ vs both floors = 0) ⇒ no source beyond re-reading-the-prompt
   + anomaly. The untried **deny-direction ABLATION also fails** (projection-out inert; additive = S3
   yes-bias). The "injection" verdict keys on off-manifoldness + prompt-inference. Pre-registration
   git-verified before results; reviewer-hardened (a GroupKFold contamination bug + an integrity

   is more internally active" (relative magnitude)** — by construction the injected concept is ALWAYS the
   more-internally-active one (proj-z ~14σ synthetic / ~4σ patch; the other ~0 even when text-emphasized),
   so a concept-aware proj-z MAGNITUDE oracle decodes which was injected at 1.0 with NO residual headroom,
   and a held-out which-of-two SOURCE probe (which DOES have power — a non-relational "injected vs none"

   The **on-manifold patch cannot even host the balanced design** (the full-context graft ERASES the

- **Segment 9 — Final synthesis, reproducibility & review.** Consolidate findings across all
